+ ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLP AXELERA AI SECURES OVER $250M IN RECORD EU AI SEMICONDUCTOR INVESTMENT

Axelera AI, an Edge AI hardware startup, has raised more than $250 million in its most recent funding round.

Orrick advised Axelera AI on the transaction.

THE COMPANY Axelera AI is a European semiconductor leader developing advanced AI acceleration hardware for edge computing and data centre applications, driving the next generation of artificial intelligence across Europe.

Source : Company Website

+ AGATHA KLUK JOINS ORRICKS NEW YORK TECH COMPANIES GROUP

Leading tech companies advisor Agatha Kluk joins Orrick as a partner in New York. Agatha is a sought-after counselor to founders, operators, board members and venture investors in the NY tech ecosystem. She advises clients throughout the lifecycle - from formation to venture financing to exit - in the consumer tech, AI, health and wellness, fintech and cleantech sectors. Agatha joins from Perkins Coie, which in 2022 combined with Kluk Farber Law, the 12-lawyer venture law boutique she founded. Agatha began her legal career at Debevoise & Plimpton.

Source : Company Website

+ ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLPREALTEK SECURES KEY ANTITRUST WIN IN SMART TV CHIP DISPUTE

Semiconductor company Realtek Semiconductor Corp. secured a key win in its dispute over a "bounty" patent licensing agreement by competitors that included a $1 million incentive to sue Realtek with the District Court of the Northern District of California denying the defendants' early motion for summary judgment. American Lawyer recognized the win in its Litigators of the Week column.

value.Jurisdiction: International; New York
value.Title: The Australian mining company that wanted to mine uranium in Salamanca has hired a former government lawyer to sue Spain for 1 billion euros

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHC-16D3-RWRF-11FB-00000-00The Australian mining company that wanted to mine uranium in Salamanca has hired a former government lawyer to sue Spain for 1 billion euros2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:03:45ZLexisNexisThe Australian mining company that wanted to mine uranium in Salamanca has hired a former government lawyer to sue Spain for 1 billion euros


Spain is facing a claim of approximately 1 billion euros before the World Bank's arbitration tribunal against the Australian mining company Berkeley Exploration Limited. The company, which alleges that the government blocked a uranium mine in Salamanca, has assembled a defense team with extensive knowledge of the Spanish legal system. According to court documents, its lawyers are the firms Riaño Abogados and Herbert Smith Freehills. At the former, José Manuel Gutiérrez Delgado serves as managing partner; he was head of the International Arbitration Department at the State General Legal Service, the unit created within the Ministry of Justice to defend against these arbitration cases. Furthermore, Herbert Smith was the first international law firm hired by Spain to defend against these disputes, prior to the creation of the department led by Gutiérrez. Until now, the funds suing Spain had hired state attorneys on leave of absence, but now they are successfully recruiting those who previously defended Spain in such proceedings.


During the Great Recession, the governments of José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero and Mariano Rajoy cut subsidies for renewable energy. International funds then filed claims against Spain in international arbitration under an international treaty, the Energy Charter, which guaranteed international investors "fair and equitable" treatment.
The claims were set to total billions and required the best lawyers. José María Segovia, who led Uría Menéndez from 2005 to 2019, recalls when the firm received a fee request to handle one of these arbitrations. "Luis de Carlos [co-director] and I decided that Uría could not handle an international arbitration against Spain in New York. It's one thing to appeal a decision by a ministry-something we do every day-and quite another to go up against the Kingdom of Spain in an international forum, with the reputational damage that entails. We thought it would be, I don't know, like suing to get Goya's painting of The Executions of May 2 or Guernica out of Spain. I think you have to have a bit of enlightened patriotism," explains Segovia.
The decision by the Uría law firm-with over 600 lawyers and one of Spain's largest firms by revenue-was not well received by some partners. The business went to other firms, mainly those with former state attorneys on leave in their ranks, who were the ones to take on these cases.
Initially, the Zapatero government hired an international firm, Herbert Smith. Herbert employs Eduardo-Soler Tappa, a former state attorney from the Secretary of State for Energy, and in 2018, José Ramón Mourenza joined the firm; he was the chief state attorney in the same office. Herbert successfully defended Spain in the PV Investors arbitration-the largest of its time-in which a group of photovoltaic investors sought approximately 2 billion euros.
But after those initial claims, and upon discovering that the State was facing an avalanche of cases, the then-Vice President, Soraya Sáenz de Santamaría, a State Attorney, ordered the creation of an international arbitration department within the State Attorney's Office-a unit that, at the time, only Canada had.
In May 2024, Berkeley announced that it was initiating arbitration against Spain and stated that it would be represented by Herbert Smith and Riaño Abogados. The company alleged that the Ministry of Ecological Transition had blocked a uranium mine in Salamanca. The mining operation had received a negative report from the Nuclear Safety Council, which even reported pressure from the company.
According to sources familiar with the proceedings, Herbert Smith had been representing Berkeley for 30 years and took on the defense because the case had nothing to do with renewable energy, which had been the focus of previous arbitrations. According to these sources, neither Soler-Tappa nor Mourenza are directly involved; instead, the case is being handled by Antonio Pastor Palomar, a professor at Rey Juan Carlos University.
Joining them in defending Berkeley is Riaño Abogados, the firm founded in 2024 by Miguel Riaño Pombo after he stepped down as president of Herbert. Riaño is one of the best-known lawyers and in 1996 served at the Ministry of the Environment as an advisor to Isabel Tocino. Shortly after founding the firm, Riaño Pombo recruited José Manuel Gutiérrez as managing partner.
At the State Legal Service, José Manuel Gutiérrez served as director of the International Arbitration Department, which Sáenz de Santamaría had ordered to be created. He led that department between 2018 and 2021. Gutiérrez is a classmate of Pablo Isla and former Madrid Regional Health Minister Manuel Lamela. "I served as State Attorney and Head of the International Arbitration Department at the State Attorney General's Office, having led more than 55 international investment arbitrations as counsel," he states on the website of the Civil and Commercial Arbitration Court.
Sources close to Gutiérrez noted that he left the International Arbitration Department in 2021 and that before moving to the private sector, he was assigned to the department dealing with the EU. They also noted that the arbitration brought by Berkeley has nothing to do with those involving renewable energy funds.
Like him, about 40% of state attorneys are on leave of absence, and many are suing the government. Senior officials on leave of absence face no legal restrictions on taking action against Spain and requesting reinstatement to a public position whenever they wish.
The result is that Herbert, the first law firm to defend Spain, and a former head of arbitration at the State Attorney's Office are leading an arbitration case against Spain for $1.2 billion (about (EURO)1 billion). And they are doing so against a decision by the Ministry of Ecological Transition, which now houses the Secretary of State for Energy, where two of those state attorneys once worked.
They are not the only senior officials hired by Berkeley in its battle against the Kingdom of Spain. Jaime García-Legaz, a secretary of state under the Rajoy government and a commercial expert on leave from the state, has been advising Berkeley since 2022.
Until now, the funds suing Spain had hired state attorneys on leave of absence. Sometimes these are Gulf sovereign wealth funds or public companies such as SWM, Munich's public utility. These high-ranking officials have succeeded in seizing Spanish assets abroad, while Madrid has aligned itself with the EU and abandoned the Energy Charter.
No government thanked Uría for his decision not to pursue these arbitration cases against Spain.

urn:contentItem:6JH5-V373-RT40-C4G8-00000-00ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - THE RECENT HEADLINES WITH PEER NEWS2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:03:45ZLexisNexisSTOCKROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - THE RECENT HEADLINES WITH PEER NEWS

DASHBOARD: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION

PermID4296528943Websitehttps://www.rockefellerfoundation.org/IndustryPhilanthropy / Global DevelopmentAddress420 Fifth Avenue, New York City, New York, U.S.

ACTIVITIES:

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION [3 News That Matters]

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION INVESTS IN WORKFORCE RESILIENCE AMID AI CHALLENGES

The Rockefeller Foundation is taking a proactive stance against the impending challenges posed by artificial intelligence on the workforce, committing significant funding to a national initiative aimed at enhancing workforce resilience. On 28th April 2026, the century-old philanthropic institution announced its strategy to address the disruption caused by automation, focusing on preventing job displacement and helping workers transition into AI-complementary roles.The initiative is designed as a grant-based program that prioritizes workforce interventions over product development. The Rockefeller Foundation's approach emphasizes the importance of program design, metrics, and evaluation. Central to this effort will be the measurement of displacement risk, deployment outcomes, and labor-market transitions, which are crucial for understanding the impact of automation on employment.Funding for the initiative is expected to come through various instruments, including grants, programmatic pilots, and partnerships with employers, training providers, and local governments. The choices made during the implementation phase will be critical in determining whether the Rockefeller Foundation's funding leads to scalable reskilling pipelines or merely funds isolated projects with limited long-term impact.This move by the Rockefeller Foundation serves as a strategic philanthropic signal rather than a regulatory or corporate initiative. By investing at this scale, the foundation aims to catalyze innovative models for retraining and public-private collaboration, complementing ongoing discussions about workforce policies such as universal basic income, sectoral training, and apprenticeship programs. This initiative is poised to increase the demand for measurable, data-driven evaluations within the skills and labor analytics ecosystem.As the Rockefeller Foundation rolls out this substantial program, stakeholders will be keenly observing its funding priorities, evaluation criteria, and partnership dynamics. Key considerations will include whether the initiative emphasizes durable credentialing, employer engagement, or short-term placement metrics. Ultimately, the decisions made now will be pivotal in determining whether this initiative can effectively influence labor-market outcomes or become primarily a source of funding for pilot programs.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION CHAMPIONS ECONOMIC PATHWAYS AT BALTIMORE EVENT

The Rockefeller Foundation is taking significant strides to address economic challenges in the United States, hosting the 'Big Bets for America: Baltimore' event that gathered over 250 leaders from various sectors. On 21st April 2026, the Foundation convened policymakers, philanthropists, and private-sector representatives in Baltimore to discuss and develop transformative solutions aimed at revitalizing the economy and fostering opportunity across communities.Key among the announcements made at the event, the Rockefeller Foundation launched a three-year, $100 million initiative designed to connect workers with good jobs in 250 of America’s most distressed communities. This effort aims to create approximately 1.6 million additional good jobs across sectors with the strongest growth potential, including healthcare, energy transition, food systems, and artificial intelligence. Dr. Rajiv J. Shah, President of the Rockefeller Foundation, emphasized the importance of restoring the promise of a stable life through hard work, underscoring the Foundation's commitment to the resilience of American workers.In addition to the job initiative, the Rockefeller Foundation announced a supplementary $12 million support for its 'Invest in Our Future' program, which focuses on mobilizing clean energy opportunities and fostering economic growth in underserved communities. Maryland Governor Wes Moore, who participated in the event, declared $1.5 million in philanthropic awards aimed at enhancing education and childcare in areas of high childhood poverty, further demonstrating the collaborative efforts being made to address systemic issues.The event also saw the unveiling of the second class of U.S. Big Bets Fellows, consisting of ten innovators dedicated to expanding workforce pathways and unlocking capital in underserved areas. Moreover, the Rockefeller Foundation's collaboration with various partners, including the Engine and the Los Angeles Cleantech Incubator, highlighted the importance of cross-sector partnerships in tackling climate and technology challenges, as well as enhancing community resilience and economic opportunities.Looking ahead, the next 'Big Bets for America' event is scheduled to take place in Cleveland on 9th June 2026, promising to further the dialogue and action initiated in Baltimore. The Rockefeller Foundation's ongoing commitment to fostering economic growth and opportunity reflects a strategic approach to addressing the pressing economic challenges faced by communities nationwide.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION'S SIMON WINTER ADVOCATES FOR UNIFIED DATA AT ONE HEALTH SUMMIT

Simon Winter, Vice President of the Rockefeller Foundation for Reimagining Humanitarian Nutrition Security, emphasized the urgent need for data unification at the One Health Summit. Speaking on April 21st, 2026, Winter addressed the growing challenge of fragmented data systems across humanitarian, nutritional, and climate sectors, which he believes hinders effective decision-making and resource allocation.During the panel discussion titled "Philanthropies for One Health, One Health for Children," Winter articulated that despite having access to unprecedented amounts of data, it remains trapped in silos. He noted, "We have more data than we’ve ever had in history and it’s increasingly becoming more affordable in its access, and yet it’s stuck in silos." His remarks reflect a broader concern about the inefficiencies within various data systems that serve humanitarian and environmental needs.Winter's perspective resonates with earlier comments made by Oracle co-founder Larry Ellison at the World Governments Summit in February 2025. Ellison urged for a unified platform to consolidate health data across nations, asserting that the fragmented nature of data—scattered across approximately 3,000 databases—limits its utility for AI and decision-making. The Rockefeller Foundation's call for data integration mirrors this sentiment, advocating for collaboration among local and international stakeholders.To illustrate the potential benefits of unified data, Winter cited the ongoing drought crisis in Somalia. The Rockefeller Foundation is funding a local tech incubator to create a food and nutrition systems dashboard that integrates various data sets. This tool aims to provide timely information to local communities and international funders, including the Somali diaspora, which contributes around $2 billion annually to support local farmers and pastoralists.Winter concluded his address by emphasizing the necessity of empowering local stakeholders with access to global data products, which can be combined with local information. This approach, he believes, could lead to a more effective and cost-efficient solution to the complex challenges posed by fragmented data systems, ultimately enhancing humanitarian responses in crisis situations.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FORD FOUNDATION [3 News That Matters]

+ FORD FOUNDATION RECOGNIZED BY NHRC FOR COMMITMENT TO HUMAN RIGHTS IN NIGERIA

The National Human Rights Commission (NHRC) has honored the Ford Foundation for its sustained contributions to advancing human rights in Nigeria, reinforcing a partnership that has played a critical role in strengthening access to justice and institutional capacity. On 28th April 2026, during a ceremony in Abuja, the NHRC presented an Award of Appreciation to the Foundation in recognition of its longstanding support.The award was received by the Foundation’s Regional Director for West Africa, ChiChi Aniagolu-Okoye, during a high-level engagement with directors and senior officials of the Commission. Speaking at the event, the Executive Secretary of the NHRC, Dr. Tony Ojukwu, commended the Ford Foundation’s consistent backing, particularly in initiatives aimed at expanding access to justice and strengthening the Commission’s operational capacity.Dr. Ojukwu emphasized that given the breadth and complexity of human rights challenges in Nigeria, the Commission cannot function in isolation. He stated that sustained collaboration with development partners remains essential to transforming human rights protection from policy intentions into tangible outcomes for citizens.In her remarks, Aniagolu-Okoye described the recognition as both significant and encouraging, reaffirming the Foundation’s commitment to promoting human rights. She noted that a key focus of the Foundation’s work is ensuring that citizens are not only aware of their rights but are also supported in seeking justice when those rights are violated. However, she emphasized that awareness alone is insufficient without strong and effective enforcement systems.The engagement underscored the importance of continued cooperation between the Commission and its partners in addressing evolving human rights challenges across the country. In a vote of thanks, Senior Human Rights Adviser to the Commission, Hilary Ogbonna, expressed appreciation for the Ford Foundation’s enduring partnership, highlighting key institutional advancements, including initiatives such as the MOVE project and the Commission’s dashboard system, which benefitted from the Foundation’s early support.

+ FORD FOUNDATION URGES ECOWAS FIRST LADIES TO ADDRESS GENDER-BASED VIOLENCE

The Ford Foundation has called on First Ladies across the ECOWAS region to deploy their influence more strategically in tackling gender-based violence (GBV). The Foundation described the First Ladies’ 'soft power' as a critical driver of social change and institutional accountability. This call was made by ChiChi Aniagolu, West Africa Regional Director of the Ford Foundation, while delivering a goodwill message at the ECOWAS First Ladies’ Forum on Zero Tolerance for Gender-Based Violence, held in Banjul under the SWEDD+ platform in partnership with the World Bank and the United Nations Population Fund on 24th April 2026.Aniagolu warned that despite existing frameworks and commitments, gender-based violence remains widespread, with one in three women globally affected. In West and Central Africa, she noted, harmful practices such as child marriage persist, with over 30 percent of girls in some countries married before the age of 18. She also highlighted the economic burden of GBV, estimating losses of up to 3.7 percent of Gross Domestic Product in affected countries — figures that exceed investments in key sectors such as education.While the Ford Foundation acknowledged that First Ladies are not formal policymakers, it stressed their significant role in shaping public discourse and influencing decision-making across the continent. Aniagolu cited examples of impactful First Ladies, including Maryam Babangida, whose Better Life for Rural Women Programme elevated rural women's issues into national discussions, and Fatima Bio, whose 'Hands Off Our Girls' campaign contributed to a national emergency declaration on rape in 2019. Such instances illustrate how 'soft power,' when utilized effectively, can instigate systemic change.The Ford Foundation has continued to invest in strengthening GBV prevention and response systems across West Africa, including partnerships involving First Ladies and governors’ spouses in Nigeria. These interventions have aimed to advance advocacy efforts, improve evidence-based response systems, and address harmful social norms. Aniagolu emphasized that laws and policies are insufficient without public trust and accessibility, pointing out the persistent silence among survivors driven by stigma, fear, and weak institutional confidence.In light of recent regional initiatives such as the GBV Roadmap (2026–2028), Legal Scorecard, and Joint Communiqu , Aniagolu urged First Ladies to move beyond mere commitments by promoting safe reporting, challenging harmful norms, and strengthening accountability systems. 'No girl should grow up believing silence is her only option, and no woman should be denied justice because of where she lives,' she asserted, reaffirming the Ford Foundation’s commitment to supporting advocacy, strengthening systems, and amplifying women’s voices across the region.

+ FORD FOUNDATION ADVOCATES FOR WOMEN-CENTRIC JUSTICE REFORMS IN NIGERIA

In a recent dialogue organized by the Ford Foundation, stakeholders and gender advocates emphasized the urgent need for a comprehensive redesign of Nigeria's justice system to better serve women and girls. The event, held on the sidelines of CSW70 in New York, took place on the 16th of April, 2026, and focused on the effectiveness of the nation’s plural justice system.The discussion extended beyond legal frameworks to address the lived realities of survivors, highlighting that justice is only achievable when legal literacy is enhanced and economic and cultural barriers that silence women are dismantled. Nigeria's justice landscape is shaped by statutory, customary, and religious systems, which ideally should work in harmony. However, in practice, many women fall through the gaps of these systems, often facing costly and protracted legal processes.His Royal Majesty, Obi Benjamin Ikenchukwu Keagboruzi, the Dein of Agbor, shared insights on the practical obstacles hindering women's access to formal justice. He pointed out that court fees, legal representation, and lost work hours cumulatively push justice further out of reach for many women. As a result, women often resort to more accessible customary systems, which may not provide adequate protection.Justice Bukunola Adebiyi of the Lagos State High Court echoed similar sentiments, stressing the importance of strengthening investigative and evidentiary processes to ensure justice is delivered effectively. The dialogue also featured poignant reflections from Ngozi Valentina Enih, Commissioner for Children, Gender Affairs, and Social Development in Enugu State, who, as a survivor herself, highlighted the devastating trade-offs families face when seeking justice.Elsa Stamatopoulou, an Adjunct Professor of Anthropology, offered a nuanced perspective on cultural barriers, suggesting they can also serve as resources for empowerment. Moderator Professor Joy Ezeilo, Executive Director of the Women’s Aid Collective (WACOL), concluded that Nigeria's plural legal system presents not a weakness but an opportunity for comprehensive reforms. Dr. ChiChi Aniagolu-Okoye, Regional Director for West Africa at the Ford Foundation, reiterated that genuine progress will come from understanding and addressing the realities faced by women and girls in Nigeria.

urn:contentItem:6JH6-TKT3-S1N0-G0G8-00000-00PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - NEWS RECENT INCLUDING PEERS2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:03:45ZLexisNexisSTOCKPILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - NEWS RECENT INCLUDING PEERS

DASHBOARD: PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP

PermID4296383614Websitewww.pillsburylaw.com/en/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress31 West 52nd Street, New York, NY 10019 USA

ACTIVITIES:

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, founded in 1868, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 700 lawyers across 20 offices, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in M&A, regulatory, and litigation. Generating over $700 million annually, Pillsbury advised on 2024 renewable energy transactions, recognized by Columbia Law School. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and pro bono work, with strong associate training programs. Competing with Baker McKenzie, Pillsbury stands out for its energy and tech regulatory expertise. In 2024, it expanded its AI and ESG practices, focusing on sustainable finance. Recent initiatives include cloud-based legal tech and partnerships with nonprofits like Lawyers Alliance for New York, reinforcing its community impact.

+ PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP [1 News That Matters]

+ MORGAN LEWIS LANDS PILLSBURY VETERAN TO STRENGTHEN EMPLOYEE BENEFITS PRACTICE

Morgan Lewis & Bockius LLP has announced the addition of a prominent attorney from Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP to its Employee Benefits team, a move designed to address increasing complexities in benefits law and the growing need for specialized guidance.The attorney brings broad experience in compliance and regulatory matters across employee benefits, and is expected to enhance Morgan Lewis' ability to provide comprehensive services to clients. Arriving on 08th April 2026, the hire reinforces the firm's ongoing strategy to expand its practice areas as client needs evolve.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PERKINS COIE [3 News That Matters]

+ ASHURST-PERKINS COIE MERGER SECURES STRONG PARTNER BACKING

The merger of Ashurst and Perkins Coie into Ashurst Perkins Coie has won overwhelming support from the partnerships of both firms. The decision was confirmed after a consultation period, with the approval announced on 13th April 2026. The deal is expected to close in the third quarter of the year, subject to customary closing conditions.Perkins Coie's Mark Birnbaum said the approval reflects partners' commitment to creating a forward looking firm and a shared confidence in their ability to grow and deliver high quality legal services in a changing global market. Ashurst chief executive Paul Jenkins stressed the alignment between the two practices and that combining strengths will benefit clients.The merged firm will focus on technology, energy, infrastructure and financial services. Perkins Coie firmwide managing partner Bill Malley expressed optimism that the combination will improve the firms capacity to handle complex cross border matters. The new entity will count roughly 3,000 lawyers across 52 offices in 32 countries, with major hubs in Seattle, London, Sydney and New York.The merger first announced last year brings together two law firms with combined revenues of around USD 2.8 billion. Their lawyers have wide international experience, having worked in Brussels, Dubai, Frankfurt, Hong Kong, Paris, Seoul, Shenzhen, Singapore and Tokyo. The leadership of the new firm will be shared by Malley and Jenkins as global co-CEOs, while Ashurst global chair Karen Davies and Perkins Coie corporate practice chair Brian Eiting will serve as co-chairs.

+ PERKINS COIE AND ASHURST SEAL MERGER TO CREATE ASHURST PERKINS COIE

NEW YORK — Perkins Coie and Ashurst announced today that the partnerships of both firms have voted overwhelmingly to approve their proposed combination, reflecting strong support for a union with complementary geographic reach, sector depth, and practice strength. The date of this announcement is recorded as 2026-04-14 16:18:02.950901.Upon closing, Ashurst Perkins Coie will emerge as a globally integrated firm with combined revenues of approximately US$2.8 billion. The new entity will count roughly 3,000 lawyers across more than 50 offices, with flagship hubs in Seattle, London, Sydney, and New York. It will be noted for strengths in technology, energy and infrastructure, and financial services sectors—industries pivotal to the ongoing global economic transformation.Bill Malley, the Firmwide Managing Partner at Perkins Coie, said, 'Our partnership's approval reflects a belief that combining to form Ashurst Perkins Coie will create a truly differentiated global legal platform—one with the scale, sector depth, and technological leadership to meet our clients' increasingly cross-border needs.' Paul Jenkins, Global CEO of Ashurst, added, 'This vote confirms the strong alignment between our firms and our joint ambition for the future,' highlighting the complementary expertise across sectors and practice areas that will benefit clients. The merger will create a compelling platform for top legal talent.The combination remains subject to customary closing conditions and is expected to finalize in the third quarter of 2026, positioning the new firm to navigate the complexities of the global market and deliver enhanced legal services.

+ PERKINS COIE OPENS SHENZHEN OFFICE, SIGNALING RETURN TO CHINA'S MARKET

Perkins Coie has opened a representative office in Shenzhen, marking its comeback in the Chinese market. The office was inaugurated on Apr. 14, 2026, following the closure of its Beijing and Shanghai outposts in 2024 and a strategic pivot toward Shenzhen to tap the city’s rapid growth as a technology and manufacturing hub. Leading the Shenzhen office are Bing Ai, chief representative and managing partner, and Wei Yuan, representative and litigation partner. Both are US-trained and bring substantial experience in advising technology clients. Ai focuses on patent procurement and portfolio management in sectors such as semiconductors and medical devices, while Yuan handles patent infringement disputes. Bill Malley, Perkins Coie's managing partner, said the Shenzhen move reinforces the firm's commitment to providing US legal expertise across borders and helping clients navigate global markets amid rising cross-border challenges. The development also unfolds as several international firms push deeper into Shenzhen's Greater Bay Area, even as Perkins Coie weighs a potential merger with UK-based Ashurst that could create a larger global platform.

+ COOLEY LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ CROSBY SECURES $60 MILLION SERIES B BACKING FROM COOLEY LLP AND LEADING FIRMS

Crosby, a contract review start-up, has raised $60 million in its latest Series B, marking the company’s third capital raise in under a year. The funding announcement was made on April 3, 2026, with Cooley LLP among the investors backing Crosby.The round was co-led by Lux Capital and Index Ventures, with participation from Sequoia, 01 Advisors and Bain Capital Ventures, along with angel investor Elad Gil. The fresh capital will support product development and scale Crosby’s platform to serve clients such as Clay, Rogo and Ramp, a financial services operations platform.Crosby fuses artificial intelligence with legal expertise to accelerate contract reviews. The company says the value of negotiated contracts has surpassed $1 billion, a marked jump from roughly $30 million when it emerged from stealth last year. In a blog post, Crosby noted, "As we’ve scaled, we’ve also started experimenting with entirely new technology experiences." The firm has rolled out simulations of counterparty responses to redlines, voice agents that negotiate on behalf of clients, and a dashboard that lets clients oversee Crosby’s work. Crosby was founded in 2024 by Ryan Daniels and John Sarihan, with Daniels a former Cooley associate and Sarihan a former Google software engineer and tech lead for Ramp.

+ COOLEY LLP EXPANDS TECHNOLOGY PRACTICE WITH FRESHFIELDS ALUMNI

Cooley LLP has announced a significant expansion of its technology practice, bringing on board several attorneys who previously worked at Freshfields Bruckhaus Deringer. This strategic move, aimed at bolstering Cooley's capabilities in the tech sector, highlights the firm's commitment to remaining competitive in an increasingly dynamic legal landscape.As of 23rd November 2025, Cooley's latest recruitment drive underscores the firm's ongoing efforts to enhance its service offerings in technology, a sector that continues to evolve rapidly. The arrival of these high-profile Freshfields Bruckhaus Deringer alumni is expected to not only strengthen Cooley's existing practice but also attract new clients seeking expert legal guidance in technology-related matters.The new team members bring a wealth of experience, having advised prominent clients on a range of legal issues, including intellectual property, data protection, and corporate transactions in the tech industry. Their expertise is anticipated to complement Cooley’s established reputation in venture capital and startup advisory services, further positioning the firm as a leading player in the technology legal sector.Cooley LLP’s decision to recruit from Freshfields reflects a broader trend within the legal industry, where firms are actively seeking to acquire top talent to respond to the demands of a fast-paced, technology-driven market. As legal challenges in the tech sector become more complex, the need for specialized knowledge and skills is paramount, making these strategic hires particularly timely.With this expansion, Cooley LLP aims to solidify its standing as a go-to destination for clients in the technology field, reinforcing its capabilities in an area that is critical for innovation and growth. As the firm integrates its new attorneys, stakeholders will be watching closely to see how these changes impact Cooley's market position and client services in the coming months.

+ COOLEY LLP'S LITIGATION PRACTICE CONFRONTS LEADERSHIP CHALLENGES AHEAD

Cooley LLP's litigation practice is in the spotlight as a finalist for The American Lawyer's General Litigation Department of the Year. As the litigation department continues to expand, Cooley aims to apply its expertise to complex legal challenges while staying true to its core values.The expansion of Cooley's litigation practice mirrors broader industry trends toward specialized legal services. + RAY THERAPEUTICS SECURES $125 MILLION SERIES B LED BY JANUS HENDERSON WITH NOVO HOLDINGS AND NORWEST AMONG BACKERS

Ray Therapeutics (RayTx) announced it closed a $125 million Series B that was upsized and oversubscribed, signaling strong demand from both new and existing investors. The round was disclosed on April 21, 2026 and was led by Janus Henderson Investors, with participation from Adage Capital Management, Franklin Templeton, Invus, and Marshall Wace.Existing backers including 4BIO Capital, Deerfield Management, and MRL Ventures Fund provided follow-on support, while Novo Holdings A/S, Norwest, and Platanus joined the financing. + LAW FIRM PROVIDES PRO BONO COUNSEL IN GROUNDBREAKING RENEWABLE ENERGY INITIATIVE

On March 25, 2026, the African Development Bank Group and Nordic Development Fund announced an $11.3 million commitment to pilot the Peace Renewable Energy Certificate (P-REC) Aggregation Facility, marking the first use of renewable energy certificates as a direct funding mechanism for a portfolio of mini-grids across 14 African nations.The facility will be co-managed by Camco Clean Energy and Energy Peace Partners (EPP). It aims to secure long-term purchase agreements with mini-grid developers in Burundi, Ethiopia, and Nigeria, delivering access to electricity for approximately 856,000 people.A pro bono legal team, including Scott Zimmermann, Nadia Senter, and Ethan Miller, supported EPP during the transaction. Their work underscores the firm's commitment to backing innovative climate solutions in regions where electricity access remains a challenge.

+ SIDLEY AUSTIN LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ SIDLEY AUSTIN ADVISES TELIX IN USD 2.1 BILLION REGENERON COLLABORATION

Sidley Austin has advised Telix Pharmaceuticals on its strategic collaboration with Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, a deal valued at up to USD 2.1 billion. The agreement will have Telix and Regeneron co-developing and co-commercialising next-generation radiopharmaceutical therapies under a 50:50 cost- and profit-sharing model, with Telix receiving USD 40 million upfront for four initial programmes. Announced on 21st April 2026, the partnership emphasizes joint governance and shared development and commercialization responsibilities designed to leverage both companies' oncology platforms. The collaboration aims to speed the advancement of radiopharmaceuticals in cancer treatment by combining Regeneron's oncology capabilities with Telix's radiopharmaceutical pipeline. Joshua Hofheimer, a partner at Sidley Austin, remarked that "These considerations affect governance, regulatory matters, and intellectual property, in particular." The deal underscores Telix's expansion strategy and reflects the growing role of law firms like Sidley Austin in guiding complex, cross-border biopharma collaborations as regulatory landscapes evolve.

Management says the recruitment of top-tier talent has broadened the firm's City teams, expanding service offerings and driving higher billable hours and client retention. + CFTC LAUNCHES INNOVATION TASK FORCE WITH SIDLEY AUSTIN AND LATHAM TO DRIVE DERIVATIVES REGULATION

The Commodity Futures Trading Commission has formed an Innovation Task Force by engaging Sidley Austin and Latham & Watkins to sharpen the regulatory framework governing the derivatives market, signaling a proactive embrace of technology and innovation in finance.In an announcement dated 11th April 2026, the commission said the task force will draw on the combined regulatory experience of Sidley and Latham to help shape policies that reflect current market realities, including digital assets, blockchain and AI. The goal is to balance effective oversight with practical pathways for innovation and to keep the United States a leader in global markets.

Apr. 23, 2026: Greenberg Traurig's Mexico City office has been named Competition and Antitrust Firm of the Year at the Legal 500 Mexico Awards 2026, announced in Mexico City last week. Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe announced the addition of Nicole Lopez to its team, naming her a leading authority in youth online safety policy and litigation. Lopez previously held a senior role at Meta and will help bolster the firm's capabilities as online safety challenges evolve. + ORRICK EXPANDS LONDON STRUCTURED FINANCE TEAM WITH CADWALADER DUO

Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe has added two structured finance lawyers from Cadwalader to its London office, lifting its Cadwalader tally to 42 since last year’s recruitment drive. The hires are Suzanne Bell, a top-ranked securitisation partner who spent more than 13 years at Cadwalader, and William Bibby, a special counsel who will be promoted to partner after arriving from Linklaters a year ago. urn:contentItem:6JH7-9DR3-S2YK-H13M-00000-00FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - RECENT NEWS FROM KKR & CO. INC. AND INDUSTRY PEERS2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:03:45ZLexisNexisSTOCKFORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - RECENT NEWS FROM KKR & CO. INC. AND INDUSTRY PEERS

DASHBOARD: FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC

PermID4295901538Websitehttps://www.fortress.com/IndustryAlternative InvestmentsAddress1345 Avenue OF The Americas Florida 46 New York, NY, 10105-4302 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Fortress Investment Group LLC, founded in 1998, is a global investment firm headquartered in New York City. Employing over 900 people, it manages $48 billion in assets across private equity, credit, and real estate. Acquired by SoftBank in 2017, Fortress operates independently, focusing on distressed assets and infrastructure. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in renewable energy projects. Its culture emphasizes risk management, innovation, and agility, with training in asset valuation and fintech. Competing with Apollo Global, Fortress stands out for its credit expertise and opportunistic investments. Recent initiatives include blockchain-based real estate funds and ESG-focused portfolios, aligning with investment trends. Fortress diversified strategy ensures resilience in volatile markets.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP BACKS PURE SALMON JAPAN WITH USD 180 MILLION PREFERRED-NOTE FINANCING

Pure Salmon Japan has secured a preferred note investment exceeding USD 180 million, led by funds managed by affiliates of Fortress Investment Group. The financing, completed on 1st April 2026, is designed to support the construction of the land-based facility and to help meet growing domestic demand for premium seafood.The deal underscores Fortress Investment Group’s longstanding presence in Japan and its willingness to provide both capital and strategic guidance to Pure Salmon Japan. The closing signals strong institutional backing for the project and aligns with Fortress’s broader focus on sustainable infrastructure.The project targets a production capacity of 10,000 tonnes and aims to become Japan’s largest land-based salmon operation. The facility will use advanced recirculating aquaculture system (RAS) technology to minimize imports and bolster food security.CEO Erol Emed said, "We are deeply encouraged by the dedication and conviction shown by our investors in supporting Pure Salmon Japan through this financing. Their commitment underscores the growing relevance of sustainable domestic seafood production in Japan." Fortress officials also noted the value of the partnership; Eric Golden, Co-Head of Asia at Fortress, called the investment a meaningful step in strengthening Japan's food-production infrastructure. As construction progresses, Pure Salmon Japan intends to pursue additional long-term equity partners to enhance operations.

+ FORTRESS CLOSES TUSCAN GARDENS PALM COAST DEAL, AGEWELL TO OPERATE AS PART OF GROWTH IN SENIOR LIVING PORTFOLIO

Fortress Investment Group LLC has announced the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast, a Class A seniors housing community in Palm Coast, Florida. The transaction, funded by Fortress affiliates, was completed on 12th March 2026, with AgeWell Solvere Living continuing to operate the property after managing it since 2023.Tuscan Gardens comprises 130 units and provides a full continuum of care, including assisted living, enhanced assisted living, and memory care.Established in 2018, the community sits on an 11-acre campus about one mile from the Atlantic Ocean, serving an affluent and growing senior population along Florida's northeast coast.Under AgeWell's leadership, occupancy and performance have improved significantly, positioning the community as a leading option in the Palm Coast market.Peter Stone, Head of Healthcare Real Estate Equity at Fortress, said: "We are pleased to expand our seniors housing portfolio through the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast. This is a high-quality asset in a supply-constrained, demographically attractive market. We look forward to working with AgeWell on the ongoing execution of the plan to deliver exceptional care and hospitality to residents and families."The acquisition underscores Fortress Investment Group's continued commitment to investing in well-located, high-quality senior housing assets that are believed to possess significant long-term growth potential. Founded in 1998, Fortress Investment Group manages $54 billion of assets on behalf of approximately 2,000 institutional clients and private investors worldwide across various investment strategies.

+ FORTRESS PROVIDES WHOLE-LOAN FINANCING FOR CITYVIEW'S 296-UNIT JASPER NEAR USC

Fortress Investment Group has closed a $96 million whole loan to Jasper, a 296-unit Class A multifamily project in Downtown Los Angeles. The transaction, completed on Feb. 9, 2026, was arranged by Fortress affiliates as part of the firm's ongoing support for large-scale real estate financings.Jasper, owned and developed by Cityview, is located near the University of Southern California. The property offers studio, one- and two-bedroom units with high-end finishes and a full suite of amenities, including affordable housing units. The loan proceeds will refinance construction debt and facilitate leasing efforts for the newly opened community.Cityview's Damian Gancman highlighted Fortress's efficient execution and collaborative approach throughout the deal. At Fortress, Noah Shore, Global Co-Head of Real Estate Debt, noted that Jasper reflects Fortress's focus on backing high-quality, newly built multifamily assets backed by experienced sponsors with meaningful equity. Fortress Investment Group, founded in 1998, is a diversified global investment manager with about $54 billion in assets under management as of Sept. 30, 2025, pursuing credit and real estate, private equity and permanent capital investments.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KKR & CO [3 News That Matters]

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) JOINS UAE’S ALTERRA TO SCALE CLIMATE FINANCING INITIATIVES

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) Joins UAE’s ALTERRA to Scale Climate Financing InitiativesOn Apr. 22, 2026, KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) secured a commitment from ALTERRA, the UAE’s $30 billion blended finance vehicle, to strengthen its climate transition roadmap. The terms of the arrangement were not disclosed.ALTERRA launched at COP28 with UAE backing to de-risk climate investments and has already partnered with BlackRock, Brookfield Asset Management and TPG. The addition of KKR broadens ALTERRA’s investment expertise as it pursues a goal of mobilizing up to $250 billion in climate finance by 2030, with a meaningful portion directed toward developing markets.KKR’s Global Climate Transition Strategy, co-led by Charlie Gailliot and Emmanuel Lagarrigue, covers renewables, energy efficiency and sustainable fuels. The firm has more than $100 billion in assets under management and has invested over $44 billion in climate and environmental sustainability projects since 2011. Notable bets include Zenobe in the United Kingdom, EGC in Germany, Avantus in the United States and IGNIS P2X for industrial decarbonization.The partnership aims to combine ALTERRA’s global perspective with KKR’s experience in climate and infrastructure to scale proven technologies and build more resilient energy systems for the long term.

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) SUPPORTS STARITAS SPIN-OUT VIA ACCEL-KKR COLLABORATION

Apr. 22, 2026 — Accel-KKR announces its backing of Staritas, a spin-out from ECR, with support from KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) to pursue growth in digital markets. Staritas plans to operate independently to speed product development and market penetration, leveraging Accel-KKR's operational toolkit and KKR's broader technology resources. The partners expect the investment to accelerate scale and enable Staritas to expand its offerings and service capabilities with this capital and guidance. No financial terms were disclosed. Analysts note the deal aligns with rising appetite for technology-driven firms able to adapt quickly.

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) APPOINTS JOEL TRAUT TO LEAD GLOBAL REAL ESTATE CREDIT

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) Appoints Joel Traut to Lead Global Real Estate CreditKKR appoints Traut to global head of direct lending within its real estate division, effective Apr. 20, 2026, as it expands its presence in the global real estate credit market. Traut has scaled KKR Real Estate's credit business from a seed investment of $400 million in 2015 to nearly $45 billion in assets under management by end-2025, and will oversee growth across a broader geographic footprint.Paul Fine, who joined KKR in 2015, takes on the role of head of originations to succeed Traut. Fine will focus on strengthening client relationships and expanding the firm's pool of capital. KKR's capital pools have grown from two in 2017 to eight today, including commitments such as the $850 million closed for the KKR Opportunistic Real Estate Credit Fund II in February 2025.The credit team has expanded from 10 employees in 2015 to 130 today, with Traut's experience at GE Capital Real Estate guiding European real estate credit expansion, supported by Ali Imraan and a dedicated team of 25 investment and asset management professionals.The leadership changes come as KKR positions its real estate credit operations for continued growth and opportunity in the global market.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CARLYLE GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) NAMES THREE CO-PRESIDENTS TO SHARPEN EXECUTION AND STRATEGY

Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) elevates three senior executives to co-presidents under Chief Executive Officer Harvey Schwartz, effective 22nd April 2026, to bolster decision-making and streamline operations as the firm refines its strategy in a slower macro environment.The appointments signal a structural shift intended to sharpen execution across investment, fundraising, and operational priorities at Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) as rates rise and deal activity slows.Schwartz has emphasized clearer accountability and faster decisions since taking the helm, and the co-presidents model mirrors arrangements seen at other large private equity firms to share oversight of key levers.Investors will watch how the changes influence Carlyle Group's investment activity and quarterly results in the quarters ahead.

+ CARLYLE GROUP TO BUY MAJOR MAI CAPITAL STAKE VALUED AT OVER $2.8B

The Carlyle Group Inc. said on 31st March 2026 that it will acquire a majority stake in MAI Capital Management from Galway Holdings, Harvest Partners and Oak Hill Capital, in a deal valued at more than $2.8 billion. Under the terms, MAI employees will hold a meaningful minority stake and MAI will continue to operate independently under Rick Buoncore, who serves as chairman and chief executive.The investment is designed to reinforce MAI's capital base and accelerate growth across its services, including financial planning, investment management and family-office capabilities.As of Jan 1, MAI and its affiliates oversee $72.6 billion in assets across 40 offices in the United States, and Carlyle will deploy its global resources to support continuity for clients and advisors.The deal is expected to close in the second quarter of 2026, subject to regulatory approvals. Carlyle has a track record of originating, structuring and leading equity investments in wealth-management platforms. Analysts note that while Carlyle is a solid investment, some investors may prefer AI stocks with greater upside; a report on undervalued AI stocks is available.

+ CARLYLE GROUP SECURES CONTROLLING INTEREST IN MAI CAPITAL VALUED AT $2.8 BILLION

Apr. 1, 2026 — Carlyle Group L.P. has agreed to acquire a majority stake in MAI Capital Management, valuing the adviser at more than $2.8 billion. Under the deal, Carlyle will take controlling ownership while existing owners including Galway Holdings, Harvest Partners and Oak Hill Capital exit; MAI employees will retain a meaningful minority stake. Carlyle initially invested in MAI in 2021 through Galway Holdings, which had acquired the firm that year. MAI currently manages $72.6 billion in assets, operates 40 offices nationwide and employs more than 700 people, a platform the firm says supports a diversified client base across the U.S. financial advisory and asset management landscape. The transaction occurs as Carlyle has faced stock volatility, though the company points to strong liquidity and a long dividend history, with 15 straight years of payouts at roughly 3%. Analysts note Carlyle’s overall health as FAIR, while MAI’s leadership has highlighted cultural alignment as a key driver of the partnership. MAI Chairman and CEO Rick Buoncore commented, "As we looked for a long-term partner to help us achieve that vision, Carlyle stood out for its alignment with our culture and values along with their deep industry knowledge and expertise." The deal is slated to close in the second quarter of 2026, subject to customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals, including consent from the Division of Banking in South Dakota. Harvest Partners, MAI’s former sponsor, said, "We are confident that MAI is well positioned for the next stage of growth with Carlyle’s backing." Beyond MAI, Carlyle has signaled ongoing deal activity, including a defense-focused fund and a $1 billion investment in Airtel’s data center subsidiary, Nxtra Data Ltd, as well as partnerships with CVC and UBS to broaden product distribution. BMO Capital Markets recently reaffirmed an Outperform rating on Carlyle with a $65 target as investors weigh the firm’s growth trajectory.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ OAKTREE CAPITAL MANAGEMENT LP [3 News That Matters]

+ BIOXCEL AND OAKTREE EXTEND REGISTRATION RIGHTS, ISSUE PRIVATE WARRANTS

BioXcel Therapeutics, Inc. disclosed in an 8-K filing that it has entered into a Fourth Amended and Restated Registration Rights Agreement with Oaktree Capital Management and a group of institutional holders. The move aligns with the company's ongoing efforts to optimize its capital structure and provides a framework for potential future equity transactions under the arrangement disclosed on 18 April 2026. Key elements of the deal include amendments to existing terms and the issuance of new equity warrants to lenders. The Ninth Amendment to BioXcel's Credit Agreement was signed on 27 March 2026, setting the stage for these warrants. On 15 April 2026, the company issued the unregistered warrants in a private placement, a mechanism that avoided immediate Securities Act registration as part of the financing strategy. BioXcel said it would file registration statements with the SEC to enable possible resale of the warrants' underlying shares, a move designed to improve liquidity and maintain investor confidence. Lenders have indicated they intend to hold the securities for investment purposes rather than seek rapid resale, which could moderate near-term volatility in the stock price. Market participants should note the potential dilution risk if warrants are exercised, and the registration process could increase the total share count. The financing approach signals the company's liquidity needs and strategic objectives, rather than an implicit endorsement of aggressive share issuance. BioXcel also clarified that it is not classified as an emerging growth company and remains subject to full reporting and regulatory requirements.

+ OAKTREE PARTNERS WITH NORTH MILL ON $355.8 MILLION EQUIPMENT FINANCE SECURITIZATION

Oaktree Capital Management, in partnership with North Mill Equipment Finance, has closed a $355.8 million asset-backed securitization called OAKEF 2026-1. The closing occurred on 8th April 2026 and signals strong investor appetite for quality equipment finance assets.The collateral consists of equipment finance assets originally acquired from Midland Equipment Finance in a deal completed in November 2025.The offering was rated from AAA to single A and attracted peak subscriptions of approximately 6.3 times, enabling efficient execution across the capital stack. The deal delivered a weighted average spread of 99 basis points and a weighted average yield of 4.7%.Truist Securities served as the lead structuring agent, with Deutsche Bank acting as co-manager.Brendan Beer, portfolio manager for Oaktree's asset-backed finance and structured credit platform, said the market response reflected investor confidence in Oaktree's equipment strategy and highlighted the North Mill collaboration as a key driver.David Lee, chairman and CEO of North Mill Equipment Finance, stressed that the partnership demonstrates asset quality and the servicing platform's strength, noting that NMEF will continue as primary servicer for the assets.The securitization reinforces Oaktree's position in asset-backed finance and shows its ability to attract significant investor interest and structure deals that meet partner financing objectives.

+ BROOKFIELD-BACKED OAKTREE TO MEET $7.7 BILLION REDEMPTION REQUESTS AMID MARKET TURBULENCE

On 29th March 2026, Oaktree Capital Management, a Brookfield Asset Management subsidiary, said it would honor all redemption requests from its private credit fund, totaling about $7.7 billion. The withdrawals will be capped at 8.5% of net assets, about $400 million, with Brookfield contributing roughly $80 million to facilitate the redemptions.The Oaktree Strategic Credit Fund, which targets retail investors, will repurchase 6.8% of its own shares, with Brookfield buying an additional 1.7% from a single investor, according to a letter to fund investors. The fund's quarterly dividend will be reduced by two cents, to 16 cents per share.As of 23rd March 2026, the fund reported $1.8 billion in liquidity from cash and undrawn facilities.Oaktree has been selling portions of publicly traded loans and bonds this year to mitigate exposure to software investments. In the wider market, private credit managers are grappling with heightened withdrawal requests; Blackstone's $82 billion private credit fund saw repurchase requests exceed the usual 5%, prompting a board to lift the buyback to 7%.Oaktree’s decision to honor all redemptions, in contrast with some peers, could bolster confidence among investors seeking liquidity and stability.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC (NYSE:APO) POSTS 13% MINORITY STAKE IN MCKESSON MEDICAL-SURGICAL SOLUTIONS VIA $1.25 BILLION CONVERTIBLE PREFERRED EQUITY

On Apr. 20, 2026, Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) Invests $1.25 Billion in McKesson Corporation (NYSE:MCK) for a 13% stake in its Medical-Surgical Solutions division via convertible preferred equity, valuing the unit at approximately $13 billion. The move aligns with McKesson's strategy to separate the Medical-Surgical Solutions unit and provides capital to advance strategic initiatives. The convertible preferred structure allows Apollo to participate in potential upside while McKesson maintains a staged path toward the separation. The investment underscores a strategic alignment between the two firms as McKesson seeks to optimize its core businesses. Apollo Global Management oversees about $938.4 billion in assets under management and has a market capitalization near $72.06 billion, with a trailing P/E ratio of 22.58, signaling investor confidence in growth opportunities. Insider activity raises caution, with six insider sells and one insider buy over the past year, and Apollo's financial strength is rated 6 out of 10. Market participants will monitor the implications for McKesson's separation plans and for Apollo's portfolio strategy.

+ MEDICAL SURGICAL SOLUTIONS, LLC SECURES $1.25 BILLION INVESTMENT FROM APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT (NYSE:APO) THROUGH CONVERTIBLE PREFERRED SHARES

Medical Surgical Solutions, LLC has secured $1.25 billion from Apollo Funds, managed by Apollo Global Management, Inc., through the issuance of convertible preferred shares that grant Apollo a 13% ownership stake. The transaction was formalized on April 20, 2026 and remains subject to regulatory approvals and customary closing conditions. The deal values the company at a post-money of approximately $9.62 billion, with the new capital intended to enhance operational capabilities and broaden market reach.

+ APOLLO CLOSES GATEHOUSE LIVING GROUP DEAL TO BOLSTER UK BUILD-TO-RENT PLATFORM

Apollo Global Management Inc. has expanded its UK housing footprint with the acquisition of Gatehouse Living Group from Gatehouse Bank, a transaction completed on 1st April 2026 that brings Gatehouse Investment Management and Ascend Properties under Apollo.Financial terms were not disclosed.The transaction aligns with Apollo's strategy to invest in high-potential sectors and complements its existing UK housing interests, including Miller Homes and Foundation Home Loans.GLG is recognized as a leader in Build-to-Rent single-family housing, managing a portfolio of more than 10,000 properties and having acquired more than 5,000 homes.Under Apollo ownership, GLG will be led by CEO Paul Stockwell and the current management team, with Ascend Properties continuing to provide management services for external institutional portfolios and helping GLG access capital from both Apollo and third-party partners to accelerate development.Gatehouse Bank said the sale will allow it to focus on core retail growth and Shariah-compliant home finance products.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ARES MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ ARES MANAGEMENT (NYSE:ARES) EXPANDS RETAIL PLATFORM WITH US$1.7 BILLION WHITESTONE REIT PURCHASE

Ares Management (NYSE:ARES) has agreed to acquire Whitestone REIT in a transaction valued at US$1.7 billion, taking the retail-focused REIT private and expanding its footprint in necessity-based retail centers across major U.S. metro areas. The deal signals progress in Ares' strategy to deepen exposure to essential consumer needs within real estate and related assets. On Apr. 14, 2026, Ares named E.G. Morse Partner and Head of Asia Credit, and appointed Dinesh Goel and Gabriel Fong to lead the Asia Special Situations team, reflecting the firm's push to grow credit and special situations in Asia. Whitestone's inclusion strengthens Ares' position in retail real estate and aligns with a broader plan to diversify capital allocation across geographies and asset types, including private credit and real assets. Investors will assess how Whitestone integrates with Ares' existing operations and the progress of Asia fundraising as the leadership changes take shape. The deal introduces potential risks such as profit margin compression and higher leverage, while the dividend yield of 5.38% is not fully supported by earnings

+ ARES REAL ESTATE FUNDS TO BUY WHITESTONE REIT AT $19 PER SHARE, VALUED AT ABOUT $1.7 BILLION

On Apr. 10, 2026, Ares Management announced that certain Ares Real Estate funds will acquire all common shares and operating partnership units of Whitestone REIT in an all-cash transaction valued at approximately $1.7 billion, at $19.00 per share or unit. The deal will take Whitestone private and remove the stock from the NYSE. Whitestone’s portfolio includes 56 convenience-focused retail properties totaling roughly 4.9 million square feet in Sun Belt markets, including Phoenix, Austin, Dallas–Fort Worth, Houston and San Antonio. Upon closing, Whitestone will cease trading on the NYSE and operate as a private company. Whitestone CEO Dave Holeman commented, "We believe Whitestone has shown the value of high-return smaller spaces occupied by a well-diversified mix of tenants. Our investment strategy is designed to allow businesses to fuel connection and convenience within thriving, dynamic communities. We believe this transaction with Ares is a testament to the value that strategy has created for our business and, ultimately, for our shareholders." David Roth, global head of real estate strategy and growth at Ares Real Estate, added, "This transaction reflects our high conviction in New Economy real estate as today’s consumers are increasingly seeking convenient experiences for their grocery, pharmacy, healthcare, fitness, and dining needs." The firms noted that the acquisition strengthens Ares’ real estate footprint and aligns with evolving consumer preferences toward convenience and accessibility in retail.

+ ARES TO TAKE WHITESTONE REIT PRIVATE IN $1.7 BILLION ACQUISITION

Ares Management Corp. has signed a definitive merger agreement to acquire Whitestone REIT for $1.7 billion, valuing the Houston-based REIT at $19 per share and taking it private. The closing is expected in the third quarter of 2026.On April 9, 2026, Ares said the Whitestone portfolio provides a compelling platform to expand its real estate footprint with a focus on necessity-based retail centers in high-demand markets in Arizona and Texas. Whitestone owns 56 retail properties totaling about 3.9 million square feet, with properties in Phoenix, Austin, Dallas-Fort Worth, Houston and San Antonio.Whitestone’s board of trustees unanimously approved the transaction, which remains subject to customary closing conditions including shareholder approval. The deal is supported by Whitestone management and advisory teams; Whitestone is advised by BofA Securities and Jones Lang LaSalle Securities, while Ares is advised by Citigroup Global Markets Inc. and Morgan Stanley.Market data accompanying the deal showed Whitestone shares closing at $16.94 on April 8, 2026, up roughly 35 percent from a year earlier. Ares reported assets under management of about $623 billion as of December 2025. Ares shares traded around $104.83 on April 8, 2026, down from $137.75 a year earlier.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ QATAR AIRWAYS [3 News That Matters]

+ QATAR AIRWAYS CARGO REOPENS UAE ROUTES, EXPANDS FREIGHTER SCHEDULE

Qatar Airways Cargo has resumed freighter and belly operations to and from Doha after a temporary halt due to safety concerns. The updated schedule, published on 28th April 2026, confirms freighter services to Dubai World Central (DWC) twice weekly starting 26th April, and freighter flights to Sharjah (SHJ) three times weekly beginning 1st May. Daily passenger flights to Dubai (DXB) and Sharjah (SHJ) also restart, with the airline planning to lift freighter destinations to over 50 by 1st May and to expand the passenger network to more than 150 destinations by 16th June.Additionally, shipment restrictions have been lifted on certain urgent freight routes such as Victoria, Australia. Starting 1st May, Qatar Airways Cargo will offer products under special handling codes: QRLIV for fish, mammals and diplomatic cargo; and LHO for live human organs and blood. These codes are designed to support time-sensitive cargo while maintaining safety and compliance.Bookings remain available through the Digital Lounge and Qatar Airways Cargo sales teams, while standard sales channels continue to operate. The carrier emphasized that existing products remain accessible during the ramp-up.Analysts and investors are watching the expansion closely as the UAE freight network gains momentum, signaling a broader recovery for regional logistics.This resumption follows earlier safety concerns and underscores the airline's commitment to reviving connectivity and service levels in the Gulf region.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS LAUNCHES NEXT-GEN QSUITE TO LEAD PREMIUM TRAVEL

As of 23rd April 2026, Qatar Airways said the Qsuite Next Generation will debut later this year on the Airbus A350-1000, following delays in the delivery of its new Boeing 777-9 fleet. Originally launched in 2017, the Qsuite has become a hallmark of the airline's premium cabin, with features such as sliding doors, double beds, and modular seating that can accommodate families and small groups. The Next Generation will add a movable 4K OLED screen and enhanced privacy options, while sharpening space management to give travelers more personal space and flexibility during flights. Qatar is positioning the product as part of its broader premium ecosystem at Doha Hamad International Airport, where passengers enjoy dedicated check-in, fast-track security, and access to lounges. The company says the move aims to attract high-yield corporate travelers and affluent leisure passengers and to keep ahead of rivals who are also pursuing luxury cabin innovations.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS BOOSTS GLOBAL REACH AS NETWORK REBUILD GAINS MOMENTUM

Qatar Airways is continuing to lift embargoes and restore its network; by April 20, 2026, passenger operations are back to over 150 destinations, while freighter services now cover more than 50 locations.The restoration is part of a broader strategy to rebuild capacity and meet growing demand in air cargo.However, certain shipments remain restricted. Special handling codes AVF, AVM, and DOC are still embargoed, and live human organs and blood are not accepted.Customers can still book QRLIV shipments under alternate codes, as long as transit time in Doha does not exceed 24 hours.Other products are available via the Digital Lounge and local sales teams, and Qatar Airways Cargo says the network restoration reflects its adaptability. The carrier is lifting embargoes in phases, balancing safety with capacity and aiming to capture a larger share of the growing air cargo market.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

DASHBOARD: PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP

PermID4296383614Websitewww.pillsburylaw.com/en/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress31 West 52nd Street, New York, NY 10019 USA

ACTIVITIES:

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, founded in 1868, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 700 lawyers across 20 offices, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in M&A, regulatory, and litigation. Generating over $700 million annually, Pillsbury advised on 2024 renewable energy transactions, recognized by Columbia Law School. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and pro bono work, with strong associate training programs. Competing with Baker McKenzie, Pillsbury stands out for its energy and tech regulatory expertise. In 2024, it expanded its AI and ESG practices, focusing on sustainable finance. Recent initiatives include cloud-based legal tech and partnerships with nonprofits like Lawyers Alliance for New York, reinforcing its community impact.

+ PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ PILLSBURY OPENS IN BOSTON WITH A FOCUS ON TECHNOLOGY, LIFE SCIENCES AND FINANCIAL INDUSTRIES

Pillsbury is opening a new office in Boston, marking an important expansion into one of the nation's leading growth and innovation centers. The office will launch with a team of five senior attorneys and additional associates, who bring diverse transactional and litigation backgrounds.

Source: Company Website

+ PILLSBURYS MARQUEE INSURANCE RECOVERY & ADVISORY PRACTICE CONTINUES EXPANSION, WELCOMING RENOWNED POLICYHOLDER LITIGATOR BOB GILBERT

Robert J. Gilbert, a nationally recognized litigator who represents policyholders in complex insurance recovery matters, has joined Pillsbury from Latham & Watkins. He comes to the firm as one of the founding partners of Pillsbury's newly established Boston office.

Source: Company Website

+ AS NOVEL DIGITAL RIGHTS ISSUES COME TO THE FORE, PILLSBURY ADDS IP LITIGATOR ROBERT L. URIARTE IN LOS ANGELES

Pillsbury has further enhanced its expansive tech-focused capabilities with the addition of Intellectual Property litigator and trial lawyer Robert L. Uriarte as a partner in its Los Angeles office.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PERKINS COIE [3 Press Releases]

+ PERKINS COIE REPRESENTS SEDRON IN INVESTMENT FROM ARA PARTNERS OF UP TO $500 MILLION

PORTLAND, OR (April 15, 2026)-Perkins Coie announced today that it represented Sedron Technologies, a leader in advanced upcycling systems that transform waste streams into reusable water and fertilizer products, in an investment of up to $500 million from Ara Partners, a global private equity and infrastructure firm focused on industrial decarbonization.

Source: Company Website

+ PERKINS COIE STRENGTHENS PACIFIC NORTHWEST TRIAL BENCH WITH COMMERCIAL LITIGATOR CHAD COLTON

PORTLAND, OR and SEATTLE, WA (April 6, 2026)-Perkins Coie is pleased to announce that Chad Colton has joined its Commercial Litigation practice as a partner in Portland and Seattle, further strengthening the firm's trial capabilities in the Pacific Northwest.

Bringing two decades of experience, Chad represents clients in high-stakes commercial matters such as partnership, shareholder, breach of contract, IP, and licensing disputes. He also frequently serves as lead trial counsel for bet-the-company cases, often joining to advise and advocate for clients in the middle of litigation or immediately before trial.

Source: Company Website

+ PERKINS COIE RANKS 44TH ON FORTUNE'S "100 BEST COMPANIES TO WORK FOR®" LIST FOR 2026

SEATTLE (April 1, 2026)-Perkins Coie is proud to announce that it has again been named to Fortune's "100 Best Companies to Work For®" list for 2026, marking the 24th consecutive year that the firm has been featured on Fortune's prestigious list.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ COOLEY LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ COOLEY LLP VETHOS ANNOUNCES $200 MILLION IPO

Cooley advised Ethos Technologies, a top life insurance technology company on a mission to democratize access to life insurance, on its $200 million initial public offering (IPO). Ethos offered 5,127,696 shares of Class A common stock, and selling stockholders offered an additional 5,398,619 shares of Class A common stock, at a price of $19 per share. Ethos began trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market on January 29, 2026, under the ticker symbol LIFE.

Partner Matthew Bartus led the Cooley team advising Ethos since its formation in 2016, including on its $100 million Series D-1 in July 2021 and $200 million Series D in May 2021.

Lawyers Jon Avina, Jean Park, Milson Yu, Samara Zaifman, Katie Lapidus and Charlotte Yin led the Cooley team advising Ethos on its IPO.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP INFERACT ANNOUNCES $150 MILLION SEED FINANCING

Cooley advised Inferact, a startup founded by creators and core maintainers of the open-source project vLLM, on its $150 million seed financing at an $800 million valuation. The financing was co-led by Andreessen Horowitz and Lightspeed Venture Partners, with investments from Sequoia Capital, Altimeter Capital, Redpoint Ventures, ZhenFund, The House Fund, Striker Venture Partners, Laude Ventures, Databricks Ventures and the UC Berkeley Chancellor's Fund.

Lawyers Peter Werner, Tara Capsuto, Won Lee, Carl Min and Isabella Ysebrands led the Cooley team advising Inferact.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP OPENEVIDENCE RAISES $250 MILLION SERIES D

Cooley advised OpenEvidence, the most widely used artificial intelligence (AI) platform by doctors in America, on its $250 million Series D financing, valuing the company at $12 billion and making it the most valuable healthcare AI company in the world. The new funding will be used to invest heavily in the research and development and compute costs associated with the multi-AI agentic architecture of OpenEvidence, which provides the highest quality and most accurate medical answers of any system in the world.

The round was co-led by Thrive Capital and DST Global, and brings the total funding raised by OpenEvidence to nearly $700 million over the past year.

Lawyers Derek Colla, David Safren and Sela Dougherty led the Cooley team advising OpenEvidence.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP NEUROPHOS SECURES $110 MILLION SERIES A

Cooley advised Neurophos, a leader in photonic artificial intelligence chip technology, on its oversubscribed $110 million Series A round, bringing total funding to $118 million. The new funding will accelerate the delivery of Neurophos' first integrated photonic compute system, including datacenter-ready optical processing unit modules, a full software stack and early access developer hardware.

The round was led by Gates Frontier, with participation from M12 (Microsoft's venture fund), Carbon Direct Capital, Aramco Ventures, Bosch Ventures, Tectonic Ventures, Space Capital and others.

Lawyers James Allen, Nick Hobson, James Reizman and Nathan Alamillo led the Cooley team advising Neurophos.

+ REED SMITH ADVISES NATURPAK ON SALE BY TRIVEST TO PPC

CHICAGO - Global law firm Reed Smith represented Trivest Partners and IPMF, LLC, doing business as NaturPak, a manufacturer of bone broths, soups, sauces, wet pet food, and food ingredients for national retailers and brands, on its sale by Trivest Growth Investment Fund II to Pritzker Private Capital ("PPC"). Trivest Growth Investment Fund II, managed by Trivest Partners LP, a private equity firm focused exclusively on investments in founder- and family owned businesses, partnered with NaturPak through a minority investment in 2020. During Trivest's partnership, NaturPak transformed from a small specialty co-manufacturer into a scaled, multi-segment platform, increasing its R&D and manufacturing capabilities and production capacity.

+ REED SMITH PARTNERS JOSH LOVE, ERIC MARDER, STEPHEN SESSA, AND EDWARD SHAPIRO NAMED TO VARIETY'S "DEALMAKERS 2025" REPORT

LOS ANGELES - Global law firm Reed Smith announced today that Josh Love, Eric Marder, Stephen Sessa, and Edward Shapiro, partners in the firm's global Entertainment & Media Industry Group, have been named to Variety's "Dealmakers 2025" report. Love, Marder, Sessa, and Shapiro are among an elite group of entertainment attorneys who have been chosen based on their more than $3 billion in music transactions in 2025, high‑profile client roster, and leadership across the music industry. The publication highlights their work on "sports and music transactions, start-ups and brands," "recording, music publishing, and live performance agreements (including Bad Bunny's upcoming Super Bowl halftime show)," and "catalog acquisitions."
