urn:contentItem:6JH1-0XR3-RRT2-X125-00000-00PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - SECTOR SPOTLIGHT: KEY TRENDS AND PEER HIGHLIGHTS2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:03:45ZLexisNexisSTOCKPILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - SECTOR SPOTLIGHT: KEY TRENDS AND PEER HIGHLIGHTS

DASHBOARD: PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP

PermID4296383614Websitewww.pillsburylaw.com/en/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress31 West 52nd Street, New York, NY 10019 USA

ACTIVITIES:

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, founded in 1868, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 700 lawyers across 20 offices, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in M&A, regulatory, and litigation. Generating over $700 million annually, Pillsbury advised on 2024 renewable energy transactions, recognized by Columbia Law School. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and pro bono work, with strong associate training programs. Competing with Baker McKenzie, Pillsbury stands out for its energy and tech regulatory expertise. In 2024, it expanded its AI and ESG practices, focusing on sustainable finance. Recent initiatives include cloud-based legal tech and partnerships with nonprofits like Lawyers Alliance for New York, reinforcing its community impact.

+ PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ PILLSBURY ADVISES SCHEMATIC ON $6.5 MILLION SEED FUNDING ROUND

Pillsbury advised Schematic, a Boulder, Colorado-based provider of a runtime monetization platform for SaaS and AI companies, in connection with its $6.5 million seed funding round.

Source: Company Website

+ PILLSBURY ADVISES BLUEPRINT EQUITY ON K1 INVESTMENT MANAGEMENTS GROWTH INVESTMENT IN SPIN.AI

Pillsbury advised Blueprint Equity, a tech-focused growth equity firm focused on investing in and building market-leading enterprise businesses, in connection with small-cap AI-powered software investor K1 Investment Management, LLC's investment in Spin.AI.

Source: Company Website

+ PILLSBURY EXPANDS ITS MARKET-LEADING EMERGING COMPANIES & VENTURE CAPITAL PRACTICE WITH ADDITION OF PARTNERS MIKE FLYNN AND SAARAH WOODBY IN NEW YORK

Pillsbury's premier Emerging Companies & Venture Capital (ECVC) practice-ranked by LSEG among the Top 4 most active for VC financings and investor representation globally-has welcomed partners Mike Flynn and Saarah Woodby to its New York-based team. Flynn, who has previously served as a Pillsbury partner, and Woodby both join from McDermott Will & Schulte.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PERKINS COIE [3 Press Releases]

+ PERKINS COIE REPRESENTS SEDRON IN INVESTMENT FROM ARA PARTNERS OF UP TO $500 MILLION

PORTLAND, OR (April 15, 2026)-Perkins Coie announced today that it represented Sedron Technologies, a leader in advanced upcycling systems that transform waste streams into reusable water and fertilizer products, in an investment of up to $500 million from Ara Partners, a global private equity and infrastructure firm focused on industrial decarbonization.

Source: Company Website

+ PERKINS COIE STRENGTHENS PACIFIC NORTHWEST TRIAL BENCH WITH COMMERCIAL LITIGATOR CHAD COLTON

PORTLAND, OR and SEATTLE, WA (April 6, 2026)-Perkins Coie is pleased to announce that Chad Colton has joined its Commercial Litigation practice as a partner in Portland and Seattle, further strengthening the firm's trial capabilities in the Pacific Northwest.

Bringing two decades of experience, Chad represents clients in high-stakes commercial matters such as partnership, shareholder, breach of contract, IP, and licensing disputes. He also frequently serves as lead trial counsel for bet-the-company cases, often joining to advise and advocate for clients in the middle of litigation or immediately before trial.

Source: Company Website

+ PERKINS COIE RANKS 44TH ON FORTUNE'S "100 BEST COMPANIES TO WORK FOR®" LIST FOR 2026

SEATTLE (April 1, 2026)-Perkins Coie is proud to announce that it has again been named to Fortune's "100 Best Companies to Work For®" list for 2026, marking the 24th consecutive year that the firm has been featured on Fortune's prestigious list.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ COOLEY LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ COOLEY LLP VETHOS ANNOUNCES $200 MILLION IPO

Cooley advised Ethos Technologies, a top life insurance technology company on a mission to democratize access to life insurance, on its $200 million initial public offering (IPO). Ethos offered 5,127,696 shares of Class A common stock, and selling stockholders offered an additional 5,398,619 shares of Class A common stock, at a price of $19 per share. Ethos began trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market on January 29, 2026, under the ticker symbol LIFE.

Partner Matthew Bartus led the Cooley team advising Ethos since its formation in 2016, including on its $100 million Series D-1 in July 2021 and $200 million Series D in May 2021.

Lawyers Jon Avina, Jean Park, Milson Yu, Samara Zaifman, Katie Lapidus and Charlotte Yin led the Cooley team advising Ethos on its IPO.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP INFERACT ANNOUNCES $150 MILLION SEED FINANCING

Cooley advised Inferact, a startup founded by creators and core maintainers of the open-source project vLLM, on its $150 million seed financing at an $800 million valuation. The financing was co-led by Andreessen Horowitz and Lightspeed Venture Partners, with investments from Sequoia Capital, Altimeter Capital, Redpoint Ventures, ZhenFund, The House Fund, Striker Venture Partners, Laude Ventures, Databricks Ventures and the UC Berkeley Chancellor's Fund.

Lawyers Peter Werner, Tara Capsuto, Won Lee, Carl Min and Isabella Ysebrands led the Cooley team advising Inferact.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP OPENEVIDENCE RAISES $250 MILLION SERIES D

Cooley advised OpenEvidence, the most widely used artificial intelligence (AI) platform by doctors in America, on its $250 million Series D financing, valuing the company at $12 billion and making it the most valuable healthcare AI company in the world. The new funding will be used to invest heavily in the research and development and compute costs associated with the multi-AI agentic architecture of OpenEvidence, which provides the highest quality and most accurate medical answers of any system in the world.

The round was co-led by Thrive Capital and DST Global, and brings the total funding raised by OpenEvidence to nearly $700 million over the past year.

Lawyers Derek Colla, David Safren and Sela Dougherty led the Cooley team advising OpenEvidence.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP NEUROPHOS SECURES $110 MILLION SERIES A

Cooley advised Neurophos, a leader in photonic artificial intelligence chip technology, on its oversubscribed $110 million Series A round, bringing total funding to $118 million. The new funding will accelerate the delivery of Neurophos' first integrated photonic compute system, including datacenter-ready optical processing unit modules, a full software stack and early access developer hardware.

The round was led by Gates Frontier, with participation from M12 (Microsoft's venture fund), Carbon Direct Capital, Aramco Ventures, Bosch Ventures, Tectonic Ventures, Space Capital and others.

Lawyers James Allen, Nick Hobson, James Reizman and Nathan Alamillo led the Cooley team advising Neurophos.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ K&L GATES [3 Press Releases]

+ TWO K&L GATES PARTNERS AGAIN NAMED TO INDIA BUSINESS LAW JOURNALS INTERNATIONAL A-LIST 2026

K&L Gates is pleased to announce that Singapore Office Managing Partner Raja Bose and Singapore Corporate partner Meraj Noor have once again been named to India Business Law Journal's (IBLJ) International A-List. This marks the eighth consecutive year both partners have received this recognition.

IBLJ's International A-List identifies the top lawyers based outside of India who are recognized for their work on India-related matters. The A-List was compiled by IBLJ's seasoned editorial team following extensive market research, drawing on feedback from hundreds of lawyers at Indian law firms and thousands of India-focused in-house counsel within India and abroad.

The full International A-List 2026 is featured on IBLJ's website.

**All Singapore lawyers are members of K&L Gates Straits Law LLC, the Singapore office of global law firm K&L Gates. Source: Company Website

+ MORE THAN 80 K&L GATES LAWYERS ACROSS ASIA-PACIFIC RECOGNISED IN 2027 BEST LAWYERS, ONES TO WATCH EDITIONS

Best Lawyers®, a peer-review publication in the legal profession, has named more than 80 K&L Gates lawyers across the Asia-Pacific region in the 2027 editions of its guides, covering Australia, Japan, and Singapore. In addition, nearly 20 of the firm's lawyers were named as Best Lawyers: Ones to Watch® in Australia and Japan, lists that recognise lawyers early in their careers.

Alongside these recognitions, four Australian partners were recognised with "Lawyer of the Year" awards, an accolade that is particularly noteworthy, as it is only bestowed on one standout lawyer for each practice area in each jurisdiction.

The Best Lawyers® guides highlight top legal talent, selected by peers within the same geographical region and legal practice area, through millions of votes each year. Congratulations to the Lawyer of the Year award recipients below, as well as all lawyers recognised in the 2027 editions - resulting in nearly 180 total recognitions for the firm. Source: Company Website

+ K&L GATES ADVISES DOLPHIN ENTERTAINMENT, INC. ON US$15 MILLION PIPE INVESTMENT

Global law firm K&L Gates LLP advised Dolphin Entertainment, Inc., an entertainment marketing and premium content production company, in connection with a US$15 million private investment in public equity (PIPE) financing with Lincoln Park Capital, an investment group and asset management firm.

The Corporate team advising on the transaction was led by Miami partner Clayton Parker with support from Miami partner Erin Fogarty.

"This transaction underscores Dolphin Entertainment's commitment to accelerating strategic growth across its marketing and content-driven businesses. We were pleased to support them in securing investment from a partner aligned with its long-term vision," said Parker.

K&L Gates' Corporate practice is one of the most substantial in the legal industry, with hundreds of lawyers in offices across the globe providing clients with practical legal solutions in the structuring, financing, and completion of domestic, international, and cross-border transactions. Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ REED SMITH LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ REED SMITH ADVISES NATURPAK ON SALE BY TRIVEST TO PPC

CHICAGO - Global law firm Reed Smith represented Trivest Partners and IPMF, LLC, doing business as NaturPak, a manufacturer of bone broths, soups, sauces, wet pet food, and food ingredients for national retailers and brands, on its sale by Trivest Growth Investment Fund II to Pritzker Private Capital ("PPC"). Trivest Growth Investment Fund II, managed by Trivest Partners LP, a private equity firm focused exclusively on investments in founder- and family owned businesses, partnered with NaturPak through a minority investment in 2020. During Trivest's partnership, NaturPak transformed from a small specialty co-manufacturer into a scaled, multi-segment platform, increasing its R&D and manufacturing capabilities and production capacity.

The cross-office Reed Smith deal team included attorneys in Chicago, New York, Denver, Philadelphia, Dallas, Orange County (California), Pittsburgh, Washington, Houston, Tysons (Virginia), and Miami. The team was led by Nick Gibson, Kristen Capriotti, Crockett Stevenson, and Sudeep Kalkunte, with support from Constantine Karides, Brad Schmarak, David Hayes, Simon Kliegman, Ian Sherwin, Paulo McKeeby, Daniel Hagedorn, Michelle Mantine, Cori Goldberg, A.J. Wissinger, Jillian Burstein, Tyler Thompson, Mike Summers, Michael Jacobs, Brendan Leanos, Benjamin Brimeyer, Michael Galibois, Michael Lowell, Joseph Mammone Jr., Michelle DiPuma, Batool Banker, Leah Hungerman, Taber Rueter, Clirae Bourke, Michaela Mapes, Peter Loy, Talia Boiangin Behar, Elliott Race, George Avdellas, Katherine Litaker, Taylor Kollmansberger, and Nikita Bazaj.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH PARTNERS JOSH LOVE, ERIC MARDER, STEPHEN SESSA, AND EDWARD SHAPIRO NAMED TO VARIETY'S "DEALMAKERS 2025" REPORT

LOS ANGELES - Global law firm Reed Smith announced today that Josh Love, Eric Marder, Stephen Sessa, and Edward Shapiro, partners in the firm's global Entertainment & Media Industry Group, have been named to Variety's "Dealmakers 2025" report. Love, Marder, Sessa, and Shapiro are among an elite group of entertainment attorneys who have been chosen based on their more than $3 billion in music transactions in 2025, high‑profile client roster, and leadership across the music industry. The publication highlights their work on "sports and music transactions, start-ups and brands," "recording, music publishing, and live performance agreements (including Bad Bunny's upcoming Super Bowl halftime show)," and "catalog acquisitions."

This recognition reflects Reed Smith's continued leadership in high-stakes entertainment and music transactions, talent representation, and emerging legal issues at the intersection of entertainment, music, technology, and finance. The four partners were selected for their high-profile talent negotiations and representation business.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH ADVISES LEON CAPITAL ON STRATEGIC INVESTMENT IN AVANTIS GROUP

LONDON - Global law firm Reed Smith has advised funds advised by Leon Capital on the strategic investment in Avantis Group, a UK-based provider of specialist engineering services to the maritime and energy industries. The transaction, which represents the latest new investment by Leon Capital, completed on 23 December 2025. The terms of the investment are confidential.

The transaction involved the Development Bank of Wales and Avantis Group's founders, Chris David and Tom David. Avantis Group's existing management team will retain operational control of the business. Blake Morgan acted as legal counsel to the Development Bank of Wales, while Acuity Law advised the founders. AMA Capital Partners acted as corporate finance adviser to Avantis Group.

Reed Smith advised Leon Capital on all aspects of the transaction, providing comprehensive M&A support in connection with the strategic investment. The matter involved a multi-disciplinary team spanning M&A, tax, and regulatory considerations.

The investment supports Avantis Group's long-term growth strategy across maritime and energy markets, including expansion into digital infrastructure and defence, and reflects Leon Capital's confidence in the company's differentiated engineering platform and experienced management team.

For Reed Smith, the transaction represents a further example of the firm's work advising private capital sponsors on complex M&A transactions and continues to strengthen its relationship with Leon Capital as it expands its platform.

The Reed Smith team advising on the matter was led by Andrew Houghton, with support from Ciaran Sweeney, Georgia Moorhouse and Selina Cook on M&A matters; Caspar Fox and Tom Baxter on tax; and Emma Weeden on FDI and antitrust.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH STRENGTHENS ASIA-PACIFIC FINANCE AND TRANSPORTATION CAPABILITY WITH HIRE IN SINGAPORE

SINGAPORE - Global law firm Reed Smith announced the appointment of banking and finance partner Dr. Gregory Xu to its Singapore office. Xu, who joins from Stephenson Harwood, where he led the general banking and project finance offering in Southeast Asia and served on the firm's Supervisory Council, its executive committee, will join Reed Smith's Transportation Industry Group.

With over 15 years of experience, Xu advises on high-value, cross-border transactions across a wide range of sectors, including transportation - shipping, aviation, and logistics - trade, life sciences, energy, and technology. He regularly acts for financial institutions, funds, export credit agencies, leasing houses, asset owners, and Fortune 500 companies on complex financing, restructuring, and enforcement matters.

Xu has a strong track record in green and sustainability-linked lending and advises on multi-jurisdictional insolvency-related enforcement and restructuring matters. He also brings extensive experience in general corporate and commercial matters.

Richard Hakes, chair of Reed Smith's Transportation Industry Group, said: "Gregory is an outstanding addition to our team, bringing deep knowledge of ship finance, aviation finance, and banking throughout Southeast Asia. His arrival further strengthens our capabilities in Singapore and reinforces our commitment to the maritime, aviation, and broader transportation sectors across the Asia-Pacific region and beyond. Working closely with our shipping teams in Hong Kong and Shanghai and our aviation team in Singapore as part of our global Transportation Industry Group, Gregory will enhance our ability to provide seamless, cross-border support to clients operating in one of the world's most dynamic industries."

Xu commented: "Reed Smith's global platform, sector focus, and collaborative culture are hugely impressive. The firm's market-leading finance practice and its long-standing reputation in general banking, asset finance, and project finance were major draws for me. I'm delighted to be joining such a highly collegiate team with a clear strategy for growth in the region and look forward to contributing to the continued expansion of the firm's finance offering across Asia-Pacific."

Praj Samant, Reed Smith's Asia-Pacific managing partner, said: "We are delighted to welcome Gregory to the Singapore team. His arrival further strengthens our offering in one of the region's most strategically important sectors. We look forward to the valuable contributions he will bring to the firm locally and globally."

Xu is recognized as a leading practitioner by multiple legal directories. Chambers Asia-Pacific ranks him as a "Leading Individual" for Banking & Finance (India), while The Legal 500 identifies him as a "Leading Partner," "Next Generation Partner," and "Recommended" lawyer across Banking & Finance, Asset Finance, Projects, and Shipping in Singapore and in the foreign juris

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG ATTORNEYS RECOGNIZED ON 2026 VIRGINIA SUPER LAWYERS 'RISING STARS LIST

McLEAN, Va. - April 28, 2026 - Three attorneys from global law firm Greenberg Traurig, LLP were selected for inclusion on the 2026 Virginia Super Lawyers "Rising Stars" list, recognizing outstanding young lawyers across a range of practice areas.

Super Lawyers evaluates attorneys statewide across more than 70 practice areas. The "Rising Stars" designation is awarded to no more than 2.5% of lawyers practicing in each state who demonstrate outstanding professional achievement and peer recognition early in their careers. Candidates are identified through a rigorous selection process that combines peer nominations with independent research and evaluation.

Source: Company Website

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG TOKYO HOSTS ANREV BRIEFING ON REAL ASSETS, DATA CENTRES AND AI

TOKYO - April 30, 2026 - Greenberg Traurig, LLP's Tokyo office recently hosted and sponsored a briefing for the Asian Association for Investors in Non‑Listed Real Estate Vehicles (ANREV), bringing together institutional investors and industry professionals to discuss emerging trends in real assets, data centres, and artificial intelligence.

The program featured a panel discussion titled "Real Assets & New Technologies: Data Centres & AI Opportunities," with Greenberg Traurig Shareholder Joel H. Rothstein, chair of the firm's Asia Real Estate Practice, among the speakers. Panelists discussed how advances in AI are reshaping capital allocation, infrastructure planning, and investment strategies across global real asset markets, with a particular focus on the growing demand for data centre assets throughout the Asia‑Pacific region.

The round was led by SV Angel and co-founder Georges Harik, while Nvidia, Jeff Bezos, and Alphabet's VC arm, GV, among other venture capital investors, also participated.

Source : Company Website

+ GOODWIN PROCTER LLP FLOW NEUROSCIENCE ANNOUNCES FDA APPROVAL OF WORLDS FIRST AT-HOME BRAIN STIMULATION TREATMENT FOR DEPRESSION

Goodwin congratulates Flow Neuroscience on receiving FDA approval of their breakthrough at-home brain-stimulation device for the treatment of major depressive disorder (MDD). FDA approval marks the first time clinicians in the US are able to prescribe an at-home non-drug therapy as both a stand-alone and an adjunctive treatment for adults with moderate to severe depression. Goodwin's Technology and Life Sciences teams were thrilled to assist Flow Neuroscience in bringing this breakthrough device to patients in the US

Source: Company Website

+ GOODWIN GUIDES EVANTIC CAPITAL IN PARTICIPATION IN SYNTHESIAS $200 MILLION SERIES E

The Technology team advised Evantic Capital in its participation in the $200 million Series E funding round for Synthesia, the AI video platform transforming how organizations create and share knowledge.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLP AXELERA AI SECURES OVER $250M IN RECORD EU AI SEMICONDUCTOR INVESTMENT

Axelera AI, an Edge AI hardware startup, has raised more than $250 million in its most recent funding round.

Orrick advised Axelera AI on the transaction.

THE COMPANY Axelera AI is a European semiconductor leader developing advanced AI acceleration hardware for edge computing and data centre applications, driving the next generation of artificial intelligence across Europe.

Source : Company Website

+ AGATHA KLUK JOINS ORRICKS NEW YORK TECH COMPANIES GROUP

Leading tech companies advisor Agatha Kluk joins Orrick as a partner in New York. Agatha is a sought-after counselor to founders, operators, board members and venture investors in the NY tech ecosystem. She advises clients throughout the lifecycle - from formation to venture financing to exit - in the consumer tech, AI, health and wellness, fintech and cleantech sectors. Agatha joins from Perkins Coie, which in 2022 combined with Kluk Farber Law, the 12-lawyer venture law boutique she founded. Agatha began her legal career at Debevoise & Plimpton.

Source : Company Website

+ ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLPREALTEK SECURES KEY ANTITRUST WIN IN SMART TV CHIP DISPUTE

Semiconductor company Realtek Semiconductor Corp. secured a key win in its dispute over a "bounty" patent licensing agreement by competitors that included a $1 million incentive to sue Realtek with the District Court of the Northern District of California denying the defendants' early motion for summary judgment. American Lawyer recognized the win in its Litigators of the Week column.

Source : Company Website

urn:contentItem:6JGY-P943-RVSH-W07D-00000-00PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - INDUSTRY UPDATES FROM PEERS2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:03:45ZLexisNexisSTOCKPILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - INDUSTRY UPDATES FROM PEERS

DASHBOARD: PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP

PermID4296383614Websitewww.pillsburylaw.com/en/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress31 West 52nd Street, New York, NY 10019 USA

ACTIVITIES:

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, founded in 1868, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 700 lawyers across 20 offices, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in M&A, regulatory, and litigation. Generating over $700 million annually, Pillsbury advised on 2024 renewable energy transactions, recognized by Columbia Law School. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and pro bono work, with strong associate training programs. Competing with Baker McKenzie, Pillsbury stands out for its energy and tech regulatory expertise. In 2024, it expanded its AI and ESG practices, focusing on sustainable finance. Recent initiatives include cloud-based legal tech and partnerships with nonprofits like Lawyers Alliance for New York, reinforcing its community impact.

+ PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ PILLSBURY ADVISES BLUEPRINT EQUITY ON K1 INVESTMENT MANAGEMENTS GROWTH INVESTMENT IN SPIN.AI

Pillsbury advised Blueprint Equity, a tech-focused growth equity firm focused on investing in and building market-leading enterprise businesses, in connection with small-cap AI-powered software investor K1 Investment Management, LLC's investment in Spin.AI.

Source: Company Website

+ PILLSBURY ADVISES SCHEMATIC ON $6.5 MILLION SEED FUNDING ROUND

Pillsbury advised Schematic, a Boulder, Colorado-based provider of a runtime monetization platform for SaaS and AI companies, in connection with its $6.5 million seed funding round.

Source: Company Website

+ PILLSBURY EXPANDS ITS MARKET-LEADING EMERGING COMPANIES & VENTURE CAPITAL PRACTICE WITH ADDITION OF PARTNERS MIKE FLYNN AND SAARAH WOODBY IN NEW YORK

Pillsbury's premier Emerging Companies & Venture Capital (ECVC) practice-ranked by LSEG among the Top 4 most active for VC financings and investor representation globally-has welcomed partners Mike Flynn and Saarah Woodby to its New York-based team. Flynn, who has previously served as a Pillsbury partner, and Woodby both join from McDermott Will & Schulte.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PERKINS COIE [3 Press Releases]

+ PERKINS COIE REPRESENTS SEDRON IN INVESTMENT FROM ARA PARTNERS OF UP TO $500 MILLION

PORTLAND, OR (April 15, 2026)-Perkins Coie announced today that it represented Sedron Technologies, a leader in advanced upcycling systems that transform waste streams into reusable water and fertilizer products, in an investment of up to $500 million from Ara Partners, a global private equity and infrastructure firm focused on industrial decarbonization.

Source: Company Website

+ PERKINS COIE STRENGTHENS PACIFIC NORTHWEST TRIAL BENCH WITH COMMERCIAL LITIGATOR CHAD COLTON

PORTLAND, OR and SEATTLE, WA (April 6, 2026)-Perkins Coie is pleased to announce that Chad Colton has joined its Commercial Litigation practice as a partner in Portland and Seattle, further strengthening the firm's trial capabilities in the Pacific Northwest.

Bringing two decades of experience, Chad represents clients in high-stakes commercial matters such as partnership, shareholder, breach of contract, IP, and licensing disputes. He also frequently serves as lead trial counsel for bet-the-company cases, often joining to advise and advocate for clients in the middle of litigation or immediately before trial.

Source: Company Website

+ PERKINS COIE RANKS 44TH ON FORTUNE'S "100 BEST COMPANIES TO WORK FOR®" LIST FOR 2026

Cooley advised Ethos Technologies, a top life insurance technology company on a mission to democratize access to life insurance, on its $200 million initial public offering (IPO). Ethos offered 5,127,696 shares of Class A common stock, and selling stockholders offered an additional 5,398,619 shares of Class A common stock, at a price of $19 per share. + REED SMITH STRENGTHENS ASIA-PACIFIC FINANCE AND TRANSPORTATION CAPABILITY WITH HIRE IN SINGAPORE

SINGAPORE - Global law firm Reed Smith announced the appointment of banking and finance partner Dr. Gregory Xu to its Singapore office. Xu, who joins from Stephenson Harwood, where he led the general banking and project finance offering in Southeast Asia and served on the firm's Supervisory Council, its executive committee, will join Reed Smith's Transportation Industry Group.

With over 15 years of experience, Xu advises on high-value, cross-border transactions across a wide range of sectors, including transportation - shipping, aviation, and logistics - trade, life sciences, energy, and technology. He regularly acts for financial institutions, funds, export credit agencies, leasing houses, asset owners, and Fortune 500 companies on complex financing, restructuring, and enforcement matters.

Xu has a strong track record in green and sustainability-linked lending and advises on multi-jurisdictional insolvency-related enforcement and restructuring matters. He also brings extensive experience in general corporate and commercial matters.

Richard Hakes, chair of Reed Smith's Transportation Industry Group, said: "Gregory is an outstanding addition to our team, bringing deep knowledge of ship finance, aviation finance, and banking throughout Southeast Asia. His arrival further strengthens our capabilities in Singapore and reinforces our commitment to the maritime, aviation, and broader transportation sectors across the Asia-Pacific region and beyond. Working closely with our shipping teams in Hong Kong and Shanghai and our aviation team in Singapore as part of our global Transportation Industry Group, Gregory will enhance our ability to provide seamless, cross-border support to clients operating in one of the world's most dynamic industries."

Xu commented: "Reed Smith's global platform, sector focus, and collaborative culture are hugely impressive. The firm's market-leading finance practice and its long-standing reputation in general banking, asset finance, and project finance were major draws for me. I'm delighted to be joining such a highly collegiate team with a clear strategy for growth in the region and look forward to contributing to the continued expansion of the firm's finance offering across Asia-Pacific."

Praj Samant, Reed Smith's Asia-Pacific managing partner, said: "We are delighted to welcome Gregory to the Singapore team. His arrival further strengthens our offering in one of the region's most strategically important sectors. We look forward to the valuable contributions he will bring to the firm locally and globally."

Xu is recognized as a leading practitioner by multiple legal directories. Chambers Asia-Pacific ranks him as a "Leading Individual" for Banking & Finance (India), while The Legal 500 identifies him as a "Leading Partner," "Next Generation Partner," and "Recommended" lawyer across Banking & Finance, Asset Finance, Projects, and Shipping in Singapore and in the foreign juris

+ REED SMITH ADVISES LEON CAPITAL ON STRATEGIC INVESTMENT IN AVANTIS GROUP

LONDON - Global law firm Reed Smith has advised funds advised by Leon Capital on the strategic investment in Avantis Group, a UK-based provider of specialist engineering services to the maritime and energy industries. The transaction, which represents the latest new investment by Leon Capital, completed on 23 December 2025. The terms of the investment are confidential.

The transaction involved the Development Bank of Wales and Avantis Group's founders, Chris David and Tom David. Avantis Group's existing management team will retain operational control of the business. Blake Morgan acted as legal counsel to the Development Bank of Wales, while Acuity Law advised the founders. AMA Capital Partners acted as corporate finance adviser to Avantis Group.

Reed Smith advised Leon Capital on all aspects of the transaction, providing comprehensive M&A support in connection with the strategic investment. The matter involved a multi-disciplinary team spanning M&A, tax, and regulatory considerations.

The investment supports Avantis Group's long-term growth strategy across maritime and energy markets, including expansion into digital infrastructure and defence, and reflects Leon Capital's confidence in the company's differentiated engineering platform and experienced management team.

For Reed Smith, the transaction represents a further example of the firm's work advising private capital sponsors on complex M&A transactions and continues to strengthen its relationship with Leon Capital as it expands its platform.

The Reed Smith team advising on the matter was led by Andrew Houghton, with support from Ciaran Sweeney, Georgia Moorhouse and Selina Cook on M&A matters; Caspar Fox and Tom Baxter on tax; and Emma Weeden on FDI and antitrust.

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGX-2BV3-S4DG-13NW-00000-00Scott A. Edelman's Milbank Again Recognized Among Top Ten Most Prestigious Law Firms in 2027 Vault Rankings May 02, 20262026-05-03T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:03:45ZLexisNexisScott A. Edelman's Milbank Again Recognized Among Top Ten Most Prestigious Law Firms in 2027 Vault Rankings May 02, 2026

Milbank LLP is ranked 9th for overall prestige in the 2027 Vault Law Rankings, which are based on an annual survey of more than 20,000 associates.

In addition to achieving the #9 overall ranking, Milbank is rated the #1 firm for Project Finance and is among the top 10 for Clean Tech/Renewable Energy (#2), Banking & Financial Services (#5), Bankruptcy/Restructuring (#6), Energy, Oil & Gas (#6), General Corporate Practice (#9), Securities/Capital Markets (#9), Mergers & Acquisitions (#10) and Private Equity (#10). The firm is also ranked in the Securities Litigation (#19), General Commercial Litigation (#21), Business Outlook (#30) and Formal Training (#29) categories.

Source: Company Website

INDEX

SECTION 1 BIOGRAPHY: SCOTT A. EDELMAN Chairman & Partner

SECTION 2 MILBANK LLP PROFILE

SECTION 3 MILBANK LLP TOP MANAGEMENT

SECTION 1 BIOGRAPHY: SCOTT A. EDELMAN Chairman & Partner

Scott A. Edelman is Chairman of Milbank LLP and a member of the firm's Litigation & Arbitration Group. Recognized as one of the country's top litigators and corporate advisers, he specializes in high-stakes, bet-the-company litigation and trials across several substantive areas of the law, including securities, commercial and white collar.

Primary Focus & Experience

While serving as Chairman of Milbank, Mr. Edelman maintains a busy and active litigation practice, which includes trials, appeals, white collar and regulatory matters, and corporate governance issues. Over the course of his career, Mr. Edelman has taken more than 20 cases to trial and argued dozens of appeals, accumulating a slew of honors along the way. He has tried dozens of civil and criminal cases representing numerous corporations and individuals.

In 2017, Mr. Edelman was inducted as a fellow into The American College of Trial Lawyers (ACTL). Mr. Edelman has successfully led trial teams in a string of high-profile victories.

In 2024, Mr. Edelman successfully represented a criminal defendant in New York State Supreme Court. The case arose out of an allegation made by Don Henley of the Eagles that handwritten lyrics for the song "Hotel California" had been stolen from Mr. Henley. After two weeks of trial, the prosecution agreed to dismiss the case against Mr. Edelman's client.

In 2023, Mr. Edelman represented BMI (an organization that represents the interest of songwriters in the United States) in a five-week trial that resulted in BMI obtaining a 138% increase in the rate paid to songwriters by Live Nation, AEG and other live concert promoters.

In 2021, Mr. Edelman represented Auven Therapeutics in defending a billion dollar claim in which the plaintiff, Highland Capital Management, sought to invoke theories of veil piercing and alter ego to hold Auven responsible for the non-payment of notes issued by another company. Mr. Edelman successfully obtained a directed verdict against the plaintiff at the conclusion of its case.

In 2016, Mr. Edelman secured a significant victory against the DOJ on behalf of Broadcast Music, Inc. (BMI) on music licensing affecting the more than $2 billion music performing industry, which garnered him recognition by The American Lawyer as "Litigator of the Week." He also prevailed on behalf of BMI in a trial victory against Pandora in 2015 in a music licensing rate case that set the new market rate for all digital music services and awarded BMI a significant fee increase of revenue from Pandora.

On behalf of Citi Global Markets in 2014, Mr. Edelman secured an injunction preventing an arbitration under FINRA in a high-profile case brought by a Saudi investor. Mr. Edelman also won a major trial victory for Cerberus Capital Management related to the multi-billion-dollar merger with United Rentals in the first M&A litigation arising out of the troubled credit markets to proceed to trial in Delaware in 2007.

Current or Recent Representative Matters

SECTION 2 MILBANK LLP PROFILE

2.1 ACTIVITIES

Milbank LLP, founded in 1866, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 800 lawyers across 12 offices, it serves clients in finance, energy, and infrastructure, specializing in project finance, M&A, and litigation. Generating over $1 billion annually, Milbank advised on major 2024 energy deals, per its recognition by Columbia Law School. Its culture fosters collaboration, diversity, and pro bono work, with 247 attorneys registered in New York. Competing with Sullivan & Cromwell, Milbank stands out for its finance expertise, with attorneys like John D. Franchini noted for excellence. In 2024, it expanded its renewable energy and AI practices. Recent initiatives include digital transaction platforms and ESG-focused financing, aligning with market demands.

Milbank LLP's mission, vision, and values are centered around delivering exceptional results and fostering an inclusive environment. Here's a brief overview:

Mission

Milbank LLP is driven to deliver exceptional results for their clients by pushing boundaries and challenging assumptions. They aim to provide tailored solutions that exceed their clients' highest expectations.

Vision

The firm envisions itself as a leader in the legal industry, shaping the legal landscape and working with market leaders, global innovators, and paradigm-shifting entrepreneurs.

Values

Milbank LLP values excellence, diversity, and inclusion. They celebrate individuality and are committed to fostering an inclusive environment where everyone feels valued1. The firm also emphasizes professional development and building lasting relationships through various affinity groups and mentoring programs.

2.2 SUMMARY

value.Title: FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - INDUSTRY DEVELOPMENTS INCLUDING KKR & CO. INC. AND PEERS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH1-B483-RVD8-21Y3-00000-00FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - INDUSTRY DEVELOPMENTS INCLUDING KKR & CO. INC. AND PEERS2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:03:45ZLexisNexisSTOCKFORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - INDUSTRY DEVELOPMENTS INCLUDING KKR & CO. INC. AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC

PermID4295901538Websitehttps://www.fortress.com/IndustryAlternative InvestmentsAddress1345 Avenue OF The Americas Florida 46 New York, NY, 10105-4302 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Fortress Investment Group LLC, founded in 1998, is a global investment firm headquartered in New York City. Employing over 900 people, it manages $48 billion in assets across private equity, credit, and real estate. Acquired by SoftBank in 2017, Fortress operates independently, focusing on distressed assets and infrastructure. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in renewable energy projects. Its culture emphasizes risk management, innovation, and agility, with training in asset valuation and fintech. Competing with Apollo Global, Fortress stands out for its credit expertise and opportunistic investments. Recent initiatives include blockchain-based real estate funds and ESG-focused portfolios, aligning with investment trends. Fortress diversified strategy ensures resilience in volatile markets.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP BACKS PURE SALMON JAPAN WITH USD 180 MILLION PREFERRED-NOTE FINANCING

Pure Salmon Japan has secured a preferred note investment exceeding USD 180 million, led by funds managed by affiliates of Fortress Investment Group. The financing, completed on 1st April 2026, is designed to support the construction of the land-based facility and to help meet growing domestic demand for premium seafood.The deal underscores Fortress Investment Group’s longstanding presence in Japan and its willingness to provide both capital and strategic guidance to Pure Salmon Japan. The closing signals strong institutional backing for the project and aligns with Fortress’s broader focus on sustainable infrastructure.The project targets a production capacity of 10,000 tonnes and aims to become Japan’s largest land-based salmon operation. The facility will use advanced recirculating aquaculture system (RAS) technology to minimize imports and bolster food security.CEO Erol Emed said, "We are deeply encouraged by the dedication and conviction shown by our investors in supporting Pure Salmon Japan through this financing. Their commitment underscores the growing relevance of sustainable domestic seafood production in Japan." Fortress officials also noted the value of the partnership; Eric Golden, Co-Head of Asia at Fortress, called the investment a meaningful step in strengthening Japan's food-production infrastructure. As construction progresses, Pure Salmon Japan intends to pursue additional long-term equity partners to enhance operations.

+ FORTRESS CLOSES TUSCAN GARDENS PALM COAST DEAL, AGEWELL TO OPERATE AS PART OF GROWTH IN SENIOR LIVING PORTFOLIO

Fortress Investment Group LLC has announced the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast, a Class A seniors housing community in Palm Coast, Florida. The transaction, funded by Fortress affiliates, was completed on 12th March 2026, with AgeWell Solvere Living continuing to operate the property after managing it since 2023.Tuscan Gardens comprises 130 units and provides a full continuum of care, including assisted living, enhanced assisted living, and memory care.Established in 2018, the community sits on an 11-acre campus about one mile from the Atlantic Ocean, serving an affluent and growing senior population along Florida's northeast coast.Under AgeWell's leadership, occupancy and performance have improved significantly, positioning the community as a leading option in the Palm Coast market.Peter Stone, Head of Healthcare Real Estate Equity at Fortress, said: "We are pleased to expand our seniors housing portfolio through the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast. This is a high-quality asset in a supply-constrained, demographically attractive market. We look forward to working with AgeWell on the ongoing execution of the plan to deliver exceptional care and hospitality to residents and families."The acquisition underscores Fortress Investment Group's continued commitment to investing in well-located, high-quality senior housing assets that are believed to possess significant long-term growth potential. Founded in 1998, Fortress Investment Group manages $54 billion of assets on behalf of approximately 2,000 institutional clients and private investors worldwide across various investment strategies.

+ FORTRESS PROVIDES WHOLE-LOAN FINANCING FOR CITYVIEW'S 296-UNIT JASPER NEAR USC

Fortress Investment Group has closed a $96 million whole loan to Jasper, a 296-unit Class A multifamily project in Downtown Los Angeles. The transaction, completed on Feb. 9, 2026, was arranged by Fortress affiliates as part of the firm's ongoing support for large-scale real estate financings.Jasper, owned and developed by Cityview, is located near the University of Southern California. The property offers studio, one- and two-bedroom units with high-end finishes and a full suite of amenities, including affordable housing units. The loan proceeds will refinance construction debt and facilitate leasing efforts for the newly opened community.Cityview's Damian Gancman highlighted Fortress's efficient execution and collaborative approach throughout the deal. At Fortress, Noah Shore, Global Co-Head of Real Estate Debt, noted that Jasper reflects Fortress's focus on backing high-quality, newly built multifamily assets backed by experienced sponsors with meaningful equity. Fortress Investment Group, founded in 1998, is a diversified global investment manager with about $54 billion in assets under management as of Sept. 30, 2025, pursuing credit and real estate, private equity and permanent capital investments.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KKR & CO [3 News That Matters]

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) JOINS UAE’S ALTERRA TO SCALE CLIMATE FINANCING INITIATIVES

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) Joins UAE’s ALTERRA to Scale Climate Financing InitiativesOn Apr. 22, 2026, KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) secured a commitment from ALTERRA, the UAE’s $30 billion blended finance vehicle, to strengthen its climate transition roadmap. The terms of the arrangement were not disclosed.ALTERRA launched at COP28 with UAE backing to de-risk climate investments and has already partnered with BlackRock, Brookfield Asset Management and TPG. The addition of KKR broadens ALTERRA’s investment expertise as it pursues a goal of mobilizing up to $250 billion in climate finance by 2030, with a meaningful portion directed toward developing markets.KKR’s Global Climate Transition Strategy, co-led by Charlie Gailliot and Emmanuel Lagarrigue, covers renewables, energy efficiency and sustainable fuels. The firm has more than $100 billion in assets under management and has invested over $44 billion in climate and environmental sustainability projects since 2011. Notable bets include Zenobe in the United Kingdom, EGC in Germany, Avantus in the United States and IGNIS P2X for industrial decarbonization.The partnership aims to combine ALTERRA’s global perspective with KKR’s experience in climate and infrastructure to scale proven technologies and build more resilient energy systems for the long term.

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) SUPPORTS STARITAS SPIN-OUT VIA ACCEL-KKR COLLABORATION

Apr. 22, 2026 — Accel-KKR announces its backing of Staritas, a spin-out from ECR, with support from KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) to pursue growth in digital markets. Staritas plans to operate independently to speed product development and market penetration, leveraging Accel-KKR's operational toolkit and KKR's broader technology resources. The partners expect the investment to accelerate scale and enable Staritas to expand its offerings and service capabilities with this capital and guidance. No financial terms were disclosed. Analysts note the deal aligns with rising appetite for technology-driven firms able to adapt quickly.

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) APPOINTS JOEL TRAUT TO LEAD GLOBAL REAL ESTATE CREDIT

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) Appoints Joel Traut to Lead Global Real Estate CreditKKR appoints Traut to global head of direct lending within its real estate division, effective Apr. 20, 2026, as it expands its presence in the global real estate credit market. Traut has scaled KKR Real Estate's credit business from a seed investment of $400 million in 2015 to nearly $45 billion in assets under management by end-2025, and will oversee growth across a broader geographic footprint.Paul Fine, who joined KKR in 2015, takes on the role of head of originations to succeed Traut. Fine will focus on strengthening client relationships and expanding the firm's pool of capital. KKR's capital pools have grown from two in 2017 to eight today, including commitments such as the $850 million closed for the KKR Opportunistic Real Estate Credit Fund II in February 2025.The credit team has expanded from 10 employees in 2015 to 130 today, with Traut's experience at GE Capital Real Estate guiding European real estate credit expansion, supported by Ali Imraan and a dedicated team of 25 investment and asset management professionals.The leadership changes come as KKR positions its real estate credit operations for continued growth and opportunity in the global market.

+ CARLYLE GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) NAMES THREE CO-PRESIDENTS TO SHARPEN EXECUTION AND STRATEGY

Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) elevates three senior executives to co-presidents under Chief Executive Officer Harvey Schwartz, effective 22nd April 2026, to bolster decision-making and streamline operations as the firm refines its strategy in a slower macro environment.The appointments signal a structural shift intended to sharpen execution across investment, fundraising, and operational priorities at Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) as rates rise and deal activity slows.Schwartz has emphasized clearer accountability and faster decisions since taking the helm, and the co-presidents model mirrors arrangements seen at other large private equity firms to share oversight of key levers.Investors will watch how the changes influence Carlyle Group's investment activity and quarterly results in the quarters ahead.

+ CARLYLE GROUP TO BUY MAJOR MAI CAPITAL STAKE VALUED AT OVER $2.8B

The Carlyle Group Inc. said on 31st March 2026 that it will acquire a majority stake in MAI Capital Management from Galway Holdings, Harvest Partners and Oak Hill Capital, in a deal valued at more than $2.8 billion. Under the terms, MAI employees will hold a meaningful minority stake and MAI will continue to operate independently under Rick Buoncore, who serves as chairman and chief executive.The investment is designed to reinforce MAI's capital base and accelerate growth across its services, including financial planning, investment management and family-office capabilities.As of Jan 1, MAI and its affiliates oversee $72.6 billion in assets across 40 offices in the United States, and Carlyle will deploy its global resources to support continuity for clients and advisors.The deal is expected to close in the second quarter of 2026, subject to regulatory approvals. Carlyle has a track record of originating, structuring and leading equity investments in wealth-management platforms. Analysts note that while Carlyle is a solid investment, some investors may prefer AI stocks with greater upside; a report on undervalued AI stocks is available.

+ CARLYLE GROUP SECURES CONTROLLING INTEREST IN MAI CAPITAL VALUED AT $2.8 BILLION

Apr. 1, 2026 — Carlyle Group L.P. has agreed to acquire a majority stake in MAI Capital Management, valuing the adviser at more than $2.8 billion. Under the deal, Carlyle will take controlling ownership while existing owners including Galway Holdings, Harvest Partners and Oak Hill Capital exit; MAI employees will retain a meaningful minority stake. Carlyle initially invested in MAI in 2021 through Galway Holdings, which had acquired the firm that year. MAI currently manages $72.6 billion in assets, operates 40 offices nationwide and employs more than 700 people, a platform the firm says supports a diversified client base across the U.S. financial advisory and asset management landscape. The transaction occurs as Carlyle has faced stock volatility, though the company points to strong liquidity and a long dividend history, with 15 straight years of payouts at roughly 3%. Analysts note Carlyle’s overall health as FAIR, while MAI’s leadership has highlighted cultural alignment as a key driver of the partnership. MAI Chairman and CEO Rick Buoncore commented, "As we looked for a long-term partner to help us achieve that vision, Carlyle stood out for its alignment with our culture and values along with their deep industry knowledge and expertise." The deal is slated to close in the second quarter of 2026, subject to customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals, including consent from the Division of Banking in South Dakota. Harvest Partners, MAI’s former sponsor, said, "We are confident that MAI is well positioned for the next stage of growth with Carlyle’s backing." Beyond MAI, Carlyle has signaled ongoing deal activity, including a defense-focused fund and a $1 billion investment in Airtel’s data center subsidiary, Nxtra Data Ltd, as well as partnerships with CVC and UBS to broaden product distribution. BMO Capital Markets recently reaffirmed an Outperform rating on Carlyle with a $65 target as investors weigh the firm’s growth trajectory.

+ OAKTREE CAPITAL MANAGEMENT LP [3 News That Matters]

+ BIOXCEL AND OAKTREE EXTEND REGISTRATION RIGHTS, ISSUE PRIVATE WARRANTS

BioXcel Therapeutics, Inc. disclosed in an 8-K filing that it has entered into a Fourth Amended and Restated Registration Rights Agreement with Oaktree Capital Management and a group of institutional holders. The move aligns with the company's ongoing efforts to optimize its capital structure and provides a framework for potential future equity transactions under the arrangement disclosed on 18 April 2026. Key elements of the deal include amendments to existing terms and the issuance of new equity warrants to lenders. The Ninth Amendment to BioXcel's Credit Agreement was signed on 27 March 2026, setting the stage for these warrants. On 15 April 2026, the company issued the unregistered warrants in a private placement, a mechanism that avoided immediate Securities Act registration as part of the financing strategy. BioXcel said it would file registration statements with the SEC to enable possible resale of the warrants' underlying shares, a move designed to improve liquidity and maintain investor confidence. Lenders have indicated they intend to hold the securities for investment purposes rather than seek rapid resale, which could moderate near-term volatility in the stock price. Market participants should note the potential dilution risk if warrants are exercised, and the registration process could increase the total share count. The financing approach signals the company's liquidity needs and strategic objectives, rather than an implicit endorsement of aggressive share issuance. BioXcel also clarified that it is not classified as an emerging growth company and remains subject to full reporting and regulatory requirements.

+ OAKTREE PARTNERS WITH NORTH MILL ON $355.8 MILLION EQUIPMENT FINANCE SECURITIZATION

Oaktree Capital Management, in partnership with North Mill Equipment Finance, has closed a $355.8 million asset-backed securitization called OAKEF 2026-1. The closing occurred on 8th April 2026 and signals strong investor appetite for quality equipment finance assets.The collateral consists of equipment finance assets originally acquired from Midland Equipment Finance in a deal completed in November 2025.The offering was rated from AAA to single A and attracted peak subscriptions of approximately 6.3 times, enabling efficient execution across the capital stack. The deal delivered a weighted average spread of 99 basis points and a weighted average yield of 4.7%.Truist Securities served as the lead structuring agent, with Deutsche Bank acting as co-manager.Brendan Beer, portfolio manager for Oaktree's asset-backed finance and structured credit platform, said the market response reflected investor confidence in Oaktree's equipment strategy and highlighted the North Mill collaboration as a key driver.David Lee, chairman and CEO of North Mill Equipment Finance, stressed that the partnership demonstrates asset quality and the servicing platform's strength, noting that NMEF will continue as primary servicer for the assets.The securitization reinforces Oaktree's position in asset-backed finance and shows its ability to attract significant investor interest and structure deals that meet partner financing objectives.

+ BROOKFIELD-BACKED OAKTREE TO MEET $7.7 BILLION REDEMPTION REQUESTS AMID MARKET TURBULENCE

On 29th March 2026, Oaktree Capital Management, a Brookfield Asset Management subsidiary, said it would honor all redemption requests from its private credit fund, totaling about $7.7 billion. The withdrawals will be capped at 8.5% of net assets, about $400 million, with Brookfield contributing roughly $80 million to facilitate the redemptions.The Oaktree Strategic Credit Fund, which targets retail investors, will repurchase 6.8% of its own shares, with Brookfield buying an additional 1.7% from a single investor, according to a letter to fund investors. The fund's quarterly dividend will be reduced by two cents, to 16 cents per share.As of 23rd March 2026, the fund reported $1.8 billion in liquidity from cash and undrawn facilities.Oaktree has been selling portions of publicly traded loans and bonds this year to mitigate exposure to software investments. In the wider market, private credit managers are grappling with heightened withdrawal requests; Blackstone's $82 billion private credit fund saw repurchase requests exceed the usual 5%, prompting a board to lift the buyback to 7%.Oaktree’s decision to honor all redemptions, in contrast with some peers, could bolster confidence among investors seeking liquidity and stability.

Medical Surgical Solutions, LLC has secured $1.25 billion from Apollo Funds, managed by Apollo Global Management, Inc., through the issuance of convertible preferred shares that grant Apollo a 13% ownership stake. The transaction was formalized on April 20, 2026 and remains subject to regulatory approvals and customary closing conditions. The deal values the company at a post-money of approximately $9.62 billion, with the new capital intended to enhance operational capabilities and broaden market reach.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC (NYSE:APO) POSTS 13% MINORITY STAKE IN MCKESSON MEDICAL-SURGICAL SOLUTIONS VIA $1.25 BILLION CONVERTIBLE PREFERRED EQUITY

On Apr. 20, 2026, Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) Invests $1.25 Billion in McKesson Corporation (NYSE:MCK) for a 13% stake in its Medical-Surgical Solutions division via convertible preferred equity, valuing the unit at approximately $13 billion. The move aligns with McKesson's strategy to separate the Medical-Surgical Solutions unit and provides capital to advance strategic initiatives. The convertible preferred structure allows Apollo to participate in potential upside while McKesson maintains a staged path toward the separation. The investment underscores a strategic alignment between the two firms as McKesson seeks to optimize its core businesses. + APOLLO CLOSES GATEHOUSE LIVING GROUP DEAL TO BOLSTER UK BUILD-TO-RENT PLATFORM

Apollo Global Management Inc. has expanded its UK housing footprint with the acquisition of Gatehouse Living Group from Gatehouse Bank, a transaction completed on 1st April 2026 that brings Gatehouse Investment Management and Ascend Properties under Apollo.Financial terms were not disclosed.The transaction aligns with Apollo's strategy to invest in high-potential sectors and complements its existing UK housing interests, including Miller Homes and Foundation Home Loans.GLG is recognized as a leader in Build-to-Rent single-family housing, managing a portfolio of more than 10,000 properties and having acquired more than 5,000 homes.Under Apollo ownership, GLG will be led by CEO Paul Stockwell and the current management team, with Ascend Properties continuing to provide management services for external institutional portfolios and helping GLG access capital from both Apollo and third-party partners to accelerate development.Gatehouse Bank said the sale will allow it to focus on core retail growth and Shariah-compliant home finance products.

+ ARES MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ ARES MANAGEMENT (NYSE:ARES) EXPANDS RETAIL PLATFORM WITH US$1.7 BILLION WHITESTONE REIT PURCHASE

Ares Management (NYSE:ARES) has agreed to acquire Whitestone REIT in a transaction valued at US$1.7 billion, taking the retail-focused REIT private and expanding its footprint in necessity-based retail centers across major U.S. metro areas. The deal signals progress in Ares' strategy to deepen exposure to essential consumer needs within real estate and related assets. On Apr. 14, 2026, Ares named E.G. Morse Partner and Head of Asia Credit, and appointed Dinesh Goel and Gabriel Fong to lead the Asia Special Situations team, reflecting the firm's push to grow credit and special situations in Asia. Whitestone's inclusion strengthens Ares' position in retail real estate and aligns with a broader plan to diversify capital allocation across geographies and asset types, including private credit and real assets. Investors will assess how Whitestone integrates with Ares' existing operations and the progress of Asia fundraising as the leadership changes take shape. The deal introduces potential risks such as profit margin compression and higher leverage, while the dividend yield of 5.38% is not fully supported by earnings

+ ARES REAL ESTATE FUNDS TO BUY WHITESTONE REIT AT $19 PER SHARE, VALUED AT ABOUT $1.7 BILLION

On Apr. 10, 2026, Ares Management announced that certain Ares Real Estate funds will acquire all common shares and operating partnership units of Whitestone REIT in an all-cash transaction valued at approximately $1.7 billion, at $19.00 per share or unit. The deal will take Whitestone private and remove the stock from the NYSE. Whitestone’s portfolio includes 56 convenience-focused retail properties totaling roughly 4.9 million square feet in Sun Belt markets, including Phoenix, Austin, Dallas–Fort Worth, Houston and San Antonio. Upon closing, Whitestone will cease trading on the NYSE and operate as a private company. Whitestone CEO Dave Holeman commented, "We believe Whitestone has shown the value of high-return smaller spaces occupied by a well-diversified mix of tenants. Our investment strategy is designed to allow businesses to fuel connection and convenience within thriving, dynamic communities. We believe this transaction with Ares is a testament to the value that strategy has created for our business and, ultimately, for our shareholders." David Roth, global head of real estate strategy and growth at Ares Real Estate, added, "This transaction reflects our high conviction in New Economy real estate as today’s consumers are increasingly seeking convenient experiences for their grocery, pharmacy, healthcare, fitness, and dining needs." The firms noted that the acquisition strengthens Ares’ real estate footprint and aligns with evolving consumer preferences toward convenience and accessibility in retail.

+ ARES MANAGEMENT SECURES ABOUT $5.4 BILLION FOR US AND EUROPE VALUE-ADD REAL ESTATE PUSH

Ares Management Corporation (NYSE:ARES) has raised approximately $5.4 billion for value-add real estate investments in the United States and Europe, as of 9th April 2026.The funds are allocated across two main lines: about $1.9 billion for the EPEP IV fund and roughly $3.5 billion for the U.S. value-add strategy.Initial investments have been identified or deployed totaling around $1.1 billion for each track, reflecting a proactive approach to capturing growth sectors.Analysts are broadly constructive, with about 75% bullish and a consensus price target near $161, implying roughly 60% upside. On 24th March 2026, BMO Capital trimmed its target price for Ares from $140 to $112 and assigned a Market Perform rating, citing concerns over BDC redemptions, asset-based credit issues, and broader market volatility.Ares was founded as a global alternative investment manager. By end of 2025, the firm had attracted significant institutional investment, including BlackRock Inc., which holds about 7.12% stake. The report notes that while Ares presents a solid investment, some investors may seek greater upside in certain AI stocks and other sectors.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS [3 News That Matters]

+ QATAR AIRWAYS CARGO REOPENS UAE ROUTES, EXPANDS FREIGHTER SCHEDULE

Qatar Airways Cargo has resumed freighter and belly operations to and from Doha after a temporary halt due to safety concerns. The updated schedule, published on 28th April 2026, confirms freighter services to Dubai World Central (DWC) twice weekly starting 26th April, and freighter flights to Sharjah (SHJ) three times weekly beginning 1st May. Daily passenger flights to Dubai (DXB) and Sharjah (SHJ) also restart, with the airline planning to lift freighter destinations to over 50 by 1st May and to expand the passenger network to more than 150 destinations by 16th June.Additionally, shipment restrictions have been lifted on certain urgent freight routes such as Victoria, Australia. Starting 1st May, Qatar Airways Cargo will offer products under special handling codes: QRLIV for fish, mammals and diplomatic cargo; and LHO for live human organs and blood. These codes are designed to support time-sensitive cargo while maintaining safety and compliance.Bookings remain available through the Digital Lounge and Qatar Airways Cargo sales teams, while standard sales channels continue to operate. The carrier emphasized that existing products remain accessible during the ramp-up.Analysts and investors are watching the expansion closely as the UAE freight network gains momentum, signaling a broader recovery for regional logistics.This resumption follows earlier safety concerns and underscores the airline's commitment to reviving connectivity and service levels in the Gulf region.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS LAUNCHES NEXT-GEN QSUITE TO LEAD PREMIUM TRAVEL

As of 23rd April 2026, Qatar Airways said the Qsuite Next Generation will debut later this year on the Airbus A350-1000, following delays in the delivery of its new Boeing 777-9 fleet. Originally launched in 2017, the Qsuite has become a hallmark of the airline's premium cabin, with features such as sliding doors, double beds, and modular seating that can accommodate families and small groups. The Next Generation will add a movable 4K OLED screen and enhanced privacy options, while sharpening space management to give travelers more personal space and flexibility during flights. Qatar is positioning the product as part of its broader premium ecosystem at Doha Hamad International Airport, where passengers enjoy dedicated check-in, fast-track security, and access to lounges. The company says the move aims to attract high-yield corporate travelers and affluent leisure passengers and to keep ahead of rivals who are also pursuing luxury cabin innovations.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS BOOSTS GLOBAL REACH AS NETWORK REBUILD GAINS MOMENTUM

Qatar Airways is continuing to lift embargoes and restore its network; by April 20, 2026, passenger operations are back to over 150 destinations, while freighter services now cover more than 50 locations.The restoration is part of a broader strategy to rebuild capacity and meet growing demand in air cargo.However, certain shipments remain restricted. Special handling codes AVF, AVM, and DOC are still embargoed, and live human organs and blood are not accepted.Customers can still book QRLIV shipments under alternate codes, as long as transit time in Doha does not exceed 24 hours.Other products are available via the Digital Lounge and local sales teams, and Qatar Airways Cargo says the network restoration reflects its adaptability. The carrier is lifting embargoes in phases, balancing safety with capacity and aiming to capture a larger share of the growing air cargo market.

urn:contentItem:6JH3-W1X3-S34C-74K4-00000-00PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - FOCUS ON THE SECTOR: TRENDS & PEER ACTIVITY2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:03:45ZLexisNexisSTOCKPILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - FOCUS ON THE SECTOR: TRENDS & PEER ACTIVITY

DASHBOARD: PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP

PermID4296383614Websitewww.pillsburylaw.com/en/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress31 West 52nd Street, New York, NY 10019 USA

ACTIVITIES:

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, founded in 1868, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 700 lawyers across 20 offices, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in M&A, regulatory, and litigation. Generating over $700 million annually, Pillsbury advised on 2024 renewable energy transactions, recognized by Columbia Law School. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and pro bono work, with strong associate training programs. Competing with Baker McKenzie, Pillsbury stands out for its energy and tech regulatory expertise. In 2024, it expanded its AI and ESG practices, focusing on sustainable finance. Recent initiatives include cloud-based legal tech and partnerships with nonprofits like Lawyers Alliance for New York, reinforcing its community impact.

+ PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ PILLSBURY ADVISES SCHEMATIC ON $6.5 MILLION SEED FUNDING ROUND

Pillsbury advised Schematic, a Boulder, Colorado-based provider of a runtime monetization platform for SaaS and AI companies, in connection with its $6.5 million seed funding round.

Source: Company Website

+ PILLSBURY ADVISES BLUEPRINT EQUITY ON K1 INVESTMENT MANAGEMENTS GROWTH INVESTMENT IN SPIN.AI

Pillsbury advised Blueprint Equity, a tech-focused growth equity firm focused on investing in and building market-leading enterprise businesses, in connection with small-cap AI-powered software investor K1 Investment Management, LLC's investment in Spin.AI.

Source: Company Website

+ PILLSBURY EXPANDS ITS MARKET-LEADING EMERGING COMPANIES & VENTURE CAPITAL PRACTICE WITH ADDITION OF PARTNERS MIKE FLYNN AND SAARAH WOODBY IN NEW YORK

Pillsbury's premier Emerging Companies & Venture Capital (ECVC) practice-ranked by LSEG among the Top 4 most active for VC financings and investor representation globally-has welcomed partners Mike Flynn and Saarah Woodby to its New York-based team. Flynn, who has previously served as a Pillsbury partner, and Woodby both join from McDermott Will & Schulte.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PERKINS COIE [3 Press Releases]

+ PERKINS COIE REPRESENTS SEDRON IN INVESTMENT FROM ARA PARTNERS OF UP TO $500 MILLION

PORTLAND, OR (April 15, 2026)-Perkins Coie announced today that it represented Sedron Technologies, a leader in advanced upcycling systems that transform waste streams into reusable water and fertilizer products, in an investment of up to $500 million from Ara Partners, a global private equity and infrastructure firm focused on industrial decarbonization.

Source: Company Website

+ PERKINS COIE STRENGTHENS PACIFIC NORTHWEST TRIAL BENCH WITH COMMERCIAL LITIGATOR CHAD COLTON

PORTLAND, OR and SEATTLE, WA (April 6, 2026)-Perkins Coie is pleased to announce that Chad Colton has joined its Commercial Litigation practice as a partner in Portland and Seattle, further strengthening the firm's trial capabilities in the Pacific Northwest.

Bringing two decades of experience, Chad represents clients in high-stakes commercial matters such as partnership, shareholder, breach of contract, IP, and licensing disputes. He also frequently serves as lead trial counsel for bet-the-company cases, often joining to advise and advocate for clients in the middle of litigation or immediately before trial.

Source: Company Website

+ PERKINS COIE RANKS 44TH ON FORTUNE'S "100 BEST COMPANIES TO WORK FOR®" LIST FOR 2026

SEATTLE (April 1, 2026)-Perkins Coie is proud to announce that it has again been named to Fortune's "100 Best Companies to Work For®" list for 2026, marking the 24th consecutive year that the firm has been featured on Fortune's prestigious list.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ COOLEY LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ COOLEY LLP VETHOS ANNOUNCES $200 MILLION IPO

Cooley advised Ethos Technologies, a top life insurance technology company on a mission to democratize access to life insurance, on its $200 million initial public offering (IPO). Ethos offered 5,127,696 shares of Class A common stock, and selling stockholders offered an additional 5,398,619 shares of Class A common stock, at a price of $19 per share. Ethos began trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market on January 29, 2026, under the ticker symbol LIFE.

Partner Matthew Bartus led the Cooley team advising Ethos since its formation in 2016, including on its $100 million Series D-1 in July 2021 and $200 million Series D in May 2021.

Lawyers Jon Avina, Jean Park, Milson Yu, Samara Zaifman, Katie Lapidus and Charlotte Yin led the Cooley team advising Ethos on its IPO.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP INFERACT ANNOUNCES $150 MILLION SEED FINANCING

Cooley advised Inferact, a startup founded by creators and core maintainers of the open-source project vLLM, on its $150 million seed financing at an $800 million valuation. The financing was co-led by Andreessen Horowitz and Lightspeed Venture Partners, with investments from Sequoia Capital, Altimeter Capital, Redpoint Ventures, ZhenFund, The House Fund, Striker Venture Partners, Laude Ventures, Databricks Ventures and the UC Berkeley Chancellor's Fund.

Lawyers Peter Werner, Tara Capsuto, Won Lee, Carl Min and Isabella Ysebrands led the Cooley team advising Inferact.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP OPENEVIDENCE RAISES $250 MILLION SERIES D

Cooley advised OpenEvidence, the most widely used artificial intelligence (AI) platform by doctors in America, on its $250 million Series D financing, valuing the company at $12 billion and making it the most valuable healthcare AI company in the world. The new funding will be used to invest heavily in the research and development and compute costs associated with the multi-AI agentic architecture of OpenEvidence, which provides the highest quality and most accurate medical answers of any system in the world.

The round was co-led by Thrive Capital and DST Global, and brings the total funding raised by OpenEvidence to nearly $700 million over the past year.

Lawyers Derek Colla, David Safren and Sela Dougherty led the Cooley team advising OpenEvidence.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP NEUROPHOS SECURES $110 MILLION SERIES A

Cooley advised Neurophos, a leader in photonic artificial intelligence chip technology, on its oversubscribed $110 million Series A round, bringing total funding to $118 million. The new funding will accelerate the delivery of Neurophos' first integrated photonic compute system, including datacenter-ready optical processing unit modules, a full software stack and early access developer hardware.

The round was led by Gates Frontier, with participation from M12 (Microsoft's venture fund), Carbon Direct Capital, Aramco Ventures, Bosch Ventures, Tectonic Ventures, Space Capital and others.

Lawyers James Allen, Nick Hobson, James Reizman and Nathan Alamillo led the Cooley team advising Neurophos.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ K&L GATES [3 Press Releases]

+ TWO K&L GATES PARTNERS AGAIN NAMED TO INDIA BUSINESS LAW JOURNALS INTERNATIONAL A-LIST 2026

K&L Gates is pleased to announce that Singapore Office Managing Partner Raja Bose and Singapore Corporate partner Meraj Noor have once again been named to India Business Law Journal's (IBLJ) International A-List. This marks the eighth consecutive year both partners have received this recognition.

IBLJ's International A-List identifies the top lawyers based outside of India who are recognized for their work on India-related matters. The A-List was compiled by IBLJ's seasoned editorial team following extensive market research, drawing on feedback from hundreds of lawyers at Indian law firms and thousands of India-focused in-house counsel within India and abroad.

The full International A-List 2026 is featured on IBLJ's website.

**All Singapore lawyers are members of K&L Gates Straits Law LLC, the Singapore office of global law firm K&L Gates. Source: Company Website

+ MORE THAN 80 K&L GATES LAWYERS ACROSS ASIA-PACIFIC RECOGNISED IN 2027 BEST LAWYERS, ONES TO WATCH EDITIONS

Best Lawyers®, a peer-review publication in the legal profession, has named more than 80 K&L Gates lawyers across the Asia-Pacific region in the 2027 editions of its guides, covering Australia, Japan, and Singapore. In addition, nearly 20 of the firm's lawyers were named as Best Lawyers: Ones to Watch® in Australia and Japan, lists that recognise lawyers early in their careers.

Alongside these recognitions, four Australian partners were recognised with "Lawyer of the Year" awards, an accolade that is particularly noteworthy, as it is only bestowed on one standout lawyer for each practice area in each jurisdiction.

The Best Lawyers® guides highlight top legal talent, selected by peers within the same geographical region and legal practice area, through millions of votes each year. Congratulations to the Lawyer of the Year award recipients below, as well as all lawyers recognised in the 2027 editions - resulting in nearly 180 total recognitions for the firm. Source: Company Website

+ K&L GATES ADVISES DOLPHIN ENTERTAINMENT, INC. ON US$15 MILLION PIPE INVESTMENT

Global law firm K&L Gates LLP advised Dolphin Entertainment, Inc., an entertainment marketing and premium content production company, in connection with a US$15 million private investment in public equity (PIPE) financing with Lincoln Park Capital, an investment group and asset management firm.

The Corporate team advising on the transaction was led by Miami partner Clayton Parker with support from Miami partner Erin Fogarty.

"This transaction underscores Dolphin Entertainment's commitment to accelerating strategic growth across its marketing and content-driven businesses. We were pleased to support them in securing investment from a partner aligned with its long-term vision," said Parker.

K&L Gates' Corporate practice is one of the most substantial in the legal industry, with hundreds of lawyers in offices across the globe providing clients with practical legal solutions in the structuring, financing, and completion of domestic, international, and cross-border transactions. Source: Company Website
